html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 客窗闲话续集 - Collection of stories or anecdotes, a continuation of '客窗闲话' (Idle Talk in a Guestroom).
  • 难女 - Refers to a woman facing difficulties or a challenging situation, possibly metaphorical or allegorical.
  • 语怪(七则) - Tales of the Strange (Seven Pieces), indicating supernatural or mysterious stories.
  • 某官保 - Placeholder name, likely referring to an official or person of authority.
  • 黄大王 - Title possibly referring to a historical or legendary leader, deity, or powerful figure.
  • 祝由科 - Associated with traditional Chinese medicine or spiritual practices, possibly a specific school or method.
  • 李蒙师 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a teacher or mentor.
  • 唐词林 - Collection of Tang Dynasty poetry, reflecting literary heritage of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 南宋高宗遗事 - Historical events or anecdotes related to Emperor Gaozong of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 王土地 - Title possibly referring to a local deity or landowner.
  • 许湛然 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a person of significance.
  • 王理堂 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a person of significance.
  • 权阎罗王 - Refers to the god of the underworld, possibly in a context of authority or judgment.
  • 补讼师(二则) - Stories about lawyers or legal matters, reflecting the legal system of the time.
  • 粤东狱 - Specific prison or legal case in the eastern part of Guangdong province.
  • 职谬 - Mistake or misjudgment in one's professional or official capacity.
  • 智女 - Refers to a woman of intelligence or wisdom, possibly metaphorical.
  • 陆清献公遗事 - Historical events or anecdotes related to Duke Lu Qingxian.
  • 补骗子(五则) - Stories about fraud or deception, reflecting social issues of the time.
  • 奸僧狱 - Legal case involving a monk accused of misconduct, reflecting religious or moral issues.
  • 义盗 - Thief who acts out of a sense of justice or morality, possibly a complex character.
  • 悟知子 - Person who has gained wisdom or insight, possibly a figure of enlightenment.
  • 严氏 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a woman of significance.
  • 潘善人 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a person known for their virtue or goodness.
  • 身毒国 - Historical or mythical kingdom, possibly related to the region of India, reflecting cultural exchanges.
  • 某制军夫人 - Wife of a military officer, likely a figure of significance in the context of the stories.
  • 秦良玉遗事 - Historical events or anecdotes related to Lady Qin Liangyu, a famous female general.
  • 某郎中 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a physician or medical practitioner.
  • 朮芷治痘 - Specific medical treatment or practitioner, possibly reflecting medical practices of the time.
  • 乌蛇已癞 - Medical condition or metaphorical story, possibly reflecting medical or social issues.
  • 金标客 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a person of significance.
  • 文孝廉 - Placeholder name, likely referring to a person known for their filial piety and integrity.
  • 何首乌 - Medicinal plant or person with a significant name, possibly reflecting traditional Chinese medicine.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴炽昌 - Author, whose works are rooted in the cultural and historical context of late Qing China.
  • 难女 - Could be a reference to a woman in a challenging situation, possibly a metaphor for societal issues or personal struggles.
  • 语怪(七则) - Common in Chinese literature, often involving supernatural or mysterious events.
  • 某官保 - Refers to an official, possibly a figure of authority in the context of the stories.
  • 黄大王 - Title possibly referring to a historical or legendary leader, deity, or powerful figure.
  • 祝由科 - Associated with traditional Chinese medicine or spiritual practices, possibly a specific school or method.
  • 李蒙师 - Teacher or mentor, possibly a figure of wisdom or guidance in the stories.
  • 唐词林 - Collection of Tang Dynasty poetry, reflecting the literary heritage of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 南宋高宗遗事 - Historical events or anecdotes related to Emperor Gaozong of the Southern Song Dynasty, a key figure in Chinese history.
  • 王土地 - Local deity or landowner, possibly a figure of authority or reverence in the stories.
  • 许湛然 - Person of significance, possibly a figure of wisdom or virtue.
  • 王理堂 - Person of significance, possibly a figure of wisdom or virtue.
  • 权阎罗王 - God of the underworld, possibly in a context of authority or judgment.
  • 补讼师(二则) - Stories about lawyers or legal matters, reflecting the legal system of the time.
  • 粤东狱 - Specific prison or legal case in the eastern part of Guangdong province, possibly reflecting regional legal issues.
  • 职谬 - Mistake or misjudgment in one's professional or official capacity, possibly a cautionary tale.
  • 智女 - Woman of intelligence or wisdom, possibly a metaphorical figure.
  • 陆清献公遗事 - Historical events or anecdotes related to Duke Lu Qingxian, a figure of significance in Chinese history.
  • 补骗子(五则) - Stories about fraud or deception, possibly reflecting social issues of the time.
  • 奸僧狱 - Legal case involving a monk accused of misconduct, possibly reflecting religious or moral issues.
  • 义盗 - Thief who acts out of a sense of justice or morality, possibly a complex character in the stories.
  • 悟知子 - Person who has gained wisdom or insight, possibly a figure of enlightenment.
  • 严氏 - Woman of significance, possibly a figure of virtue or wisdom.
  • 潘善人 - Person known for their virtue or goodness, possibly a figure of moral integrity.
  • 身毒国 - Historical or mythical kingdom, possibly related to the region of India, reflecting cultural exchanges.
  • 某制军夫人 - Wife of a military officer, possibly a figure of significance in the context of the stories.
  • 秦良玉遗事 - Historical events or anecdotes related to Lady Qin Liangyu, a famous female general.
  • 某郎中 - Physician or medical practitioner, possibly a figure of medical expertise.
  • 朮芷治痘 - Specific medical treatment or practitioner, possibly reflecting medical practices of the time.
  • 乌蛇已癞 - Medical condition or metaphorical story, possibly reflecting medical or social issues.
  • 金标客 - Person of significance, possibly a figure of wisdom or virtue.
  • 文孝廉 - Person known for their filial piety and integrity, possibly a figure of moral integrity.
  • 何首乌 - Medicinal plant or person with a significant name, possibly reflecting traditional Chinese medicine.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一技养生 - Phrase meaning 'one skill for sustaining life,' likely referring to a specific method or practice for maintaining health.
  • 六壬神课 - Refers to a form of Chinese divination based on the *Liu Ren* system, which is a branch of the *Yi Jing* (I Ching).
  • 树上君子 - Polite way to address an intruder, meaning 'gentleman on the tree.'
  • 补明武宗遗事(三则) - Means 'supplementing the unrecorded events of Emperor Wu of the Ming Dynasty,' with '(三则)' indicating there are three stories.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明武宗 - Emperor Wu of the Ming Dynasty (1491-1521), known for his eccentric and often controversial reign.
  • 通州 - A city in present-day Beijing, historically an important administrative and commercial center.
  • 金山寺 - A famous Buddhist temple located in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 张廉访 - Likely a reference to a historical figure, possibly a censor or inspector during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 许宗伯 - A high-ranking official, possibly a Minister of Rites, involved in religious and ceremonial affairs.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴封翁 - Title referring to a retired official or a respected elder.
  • 霅溪 - Place name, possibly a river or a region in Zhejiang Province.
  • 太夫人 - Respectful term for a lady, often used to refer to a mother or a wife.
  • 邢大 - Proper name, likely a character in the story.
  • 某少君 - Placeholder for an unknown name, often used for young noblemen or officials.
  • 许宗伯 - Proper name, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 某 - Placeholder for an unknown name.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鄰人泣對曰 - Colloquial expression meaning 'the neighbor wept and replied,' indicating a deeply emotional response.
  • 素稔翁家富有 - Means 'always knew the old man's family was wealthy,' using '素稔' to convey long-standing familiarity.
  • 故行此不肖事耳 - Means 'so I did this despicable thing,' with '不肖' implying shame or disgrace.
  • 不能周濟鄰居 - Means 'unable to help the neighbor,' using '周濟' to convey assistance or support.
  • 以至為非 - Means 'leading to wrongdoing,' with '以至' indicating a causal progression.
  • 老夫之過也 - Means 'it is the old man's fault,' using '老夫' as a self-referential term of humility.
  • 汝其飽餐 - Means 'you should eat your fill,' with '其' indicating a suggestion or command.
  • 當以二十金畀汝 - Means 'I will give you twenty taels of silver,' using '畀' to convey giving or bestowing.
  • 卒歲之余 - Means 'the remainder of the year,' with '卒歲' referring to the end of the year.
  • 小作貿易 - Means 'do some small trade,' with '小作' indicating a modest or limited activity.
  • 切勿再为此事 - Means 'do not do this again,' with '切勿' emphasizing a strong prohibition.
  • 他人不汝恕也 - Means 'others will not forgive you,' using '不汝恕' to convey lack of forgiveness.
  • 一成盗名 - Means 'if you gain a reputation as a thief,' with '一成' indicating a single occurrence or establishment.
  • 则为捕役鱼肉 - Means 'you will be prey to the constables,' using '鱼肉' metaphorically to convey being exploited or victimized.
  • 没齿不能掩盖 - Means 'you will not be able to hide it for the rest of your life,' with '没齿' referring to the entire lifespan.
  • 且陷老母于不义 - Means 'and you will involve your mother in wrongdoing,' using '不义' to convey moral wrongdoing.
  • 其奈之何 - Means 'what can be done about it?' with '其奈之何' expressing a sense of helplessness or resignation.
  • 叩首无算 - Means 'knocked his head against the ground countless times,' with '无算' indicating an indefinite or large number.
  • 缘墙而去 - Means 'climbed over the wall and left,' with '缘' indicating climbing or ascending.
  • 勿使我家人知 - Means 'do not let my family know,' using '勿' to convey a prohibition.
  • 以留汝颜面 - Means 'to preserve your dignity,' with '留颜面' indicating the preservation of one's reputation or honor.
  • 方唤家人 - Means 'just as he was calling his family,' with '方' indicating the moment of action.
  • 见杯盘狼藉 - Means 'saw the tableware in disarray,' with '狼藉' indicating a mess or disorder.
  • 忽有兼人之量 - Means 'suddenly had an extra-large capacity,' using '兼人之量' to convey an unusual or exceptional capacity.
  • 且疑且喜 - Means 'both suspicious and pleased,' with '且...且...' indicating a simultaneous state of two emotions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邻人 - Refers to a neighbor, indicating a social relationship.
  • 翁 - Refers to an old man, often used as a respectful term for an elderly person.
  • 荒年 - Refers to a famine or bad harvest year, indicating a time of scarcity.
  • 卒岁 - Refers to the end of the year, indicating a time frame.
  • 二十金 - Refers to twenty taels of silver, indicating a sum of money.
  • 捕役 - Refers to constables or law enforcement, indicating authority figures.
  • 鱼肉 - Metaphorically refers to being prey or victimized, indicating a state of vulnerability.
  • 没齿 - Refers to the entire lifespan, indicating a long-term consequence.
  • 不义 - Refers to moral wrongdoing, indicating a negative ethical state.
  • 叩首 - Refers to bowing deeply or prostrating oneself, indicating a gesture of respect or submission.
  • 缘墙 - Refers to climbing over a wall, indicating a physical action.
  • 家人 - Refers to family members, indicating a social group.
  • 杯盘狼藉 - Refers to a mess or disorder, indicating a state of disarray.
  • 兼人之量 - Refers to an extra-large capacity, indicating an unusual or exceptional state.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 邻人泣对曰 - The sentence structure is straightforward but conveys a deeply emotional response.
  • 素稔翁家富有，故行此不肖事耳 - The causal relationship between knowing the old man's wealth and the neighbor's actions is clear.
  • 不能周济邻居，以致为非，老夫之过也 - The sentence structure links the inability to help the neighbor, leading to wrongdoing, and the old man's fault.
  • 汝其饱餐，当以二十金畀汝，卒岁之余，小作贸易，足以度日矣 - The sequence of actions and conditions is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
  • 切勿再为此事，他人不汝恕也 - The prohibition and its consequence are clear but require careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
  • 一成盗名，则为捕役鱼肉，没齿不能掩盖 - The sequence of actions and consequences is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
  • 且陷老母于不义，其奈之何 - The sequence of actions and consequences is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
  • 叩首无算 - The action is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the emotional impact.
  • 缘墙而去 - The action is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the physical action.
  • 勿使我家人知，以留汝颜面 - The prohibition and its purpose are clear but require careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
  • 方唤家人，见杯盘狼藉 - The sequence of actions is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
  • 忽有兼人之量，且疑且喜 - The sequence of actions and emotions is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the logical flow.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 受二品封 - Received a second-rank imperial title, indicating high honor.
  • 官至大中丞 - Reached the position of Grand Censor, the highest official rank.
  • 次公子亦入词林 - The younger son also entered the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious scholarly institution.
  • 提督学院 - Served as the supervisor of the imperial academy, a high-ranking educational official.
  • 会葬之日 - On the day of the funeral, setting the scene for the event described.
  • 祭品丰洁 - The sacrificial offerings were abundant and clean, indicating the quality and quantity of the offerings.
  • 俯伏柩前 - Kneeling before the coffin, describing the monk's respectful posture.
  • 哭之恸甚 - Cried with great sorrow, emphasizing the intensity of the monk's grief.
  • 造膝密告 - Whispered confidentially, indicating the private and intimate nature of the conversation.
  • 蒙老大人不责其罪 - Was not punished by the elder master, indicating the elder master's leniency.
  • 反以廿金为助 - Instead, gave him twenty gold coins as assistance, indicating the elder master's generosity.
  • 藉以奉母 - To support his mother, indicating the purpose of the assistance.
  • 母卒，弃去为僧 - After his mother's death, he left to become a monk, indicating the monk's transformation.
  • 苦志虔修 - Striving diligently and practicing sincerely, indicating the monk's dedication to his new life.
  • 入方丈，称大禅师 - Became the abbot and was called Great Zen Master, indicating the monk's rise to a high position in the Buddhist community.
  • 皆老大人之德有以成之 - All due to the virtue of the elder master, attributing the monk's success to the elder master's influence.
  • 不敢忘 - Will never forget, indicating the monk's gratitude.
  • 谨以实告 - Carefully and truthfully told, indicating the monk's honesty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大中丞 - Title of Grand Censor, a high-ranking official in the imperial government responsible for oversight and inspection.
  • 词林 - Term for the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution for scholars and officials in the imperial court.
  • 提督学院 - Position of supervisor of the imperial academy, a high-ranking educational official.
  • 灵隐寺 - Name of a famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 方丈 - Term for the abbot of a Buddhist temple, the highest-ranking monk.
  • 大禅师 - Title of a senior and respected Zen master in the Buddhist community.
  • 祭品丰洁 - Phrase reflecting the importance of offering clean and abundant sacrifices in traditional Chinese funerals, symbolizing respect and remembrance.

Proper Nouns

  • 长公子 - The elder son.
  • 次公子 - The younger son.
  • 翁 - The elder master.
  • 灵隐寺 - Lingyin Temple.
  • 方丈大和尚 - The abbot of Lingyin Temple.
  • 中丞 - The elder son, who held the position of Grand Censor.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此不难于济助，难于惟恐人知 - It is not difficult to help someone, but it is difficult to do so without being noticed. Highlights the importance of humility and discretion in acts of kindness.
  • 谆谆告诫，俾改行从善，是为阴德 - Repeatedly admonish or advise to encourage someone to change their ways and become good, considered acts of hidden virtue.
  • 难女 - Refers to a woman who has experienced difficulties or challenges in her life, often implying a moral or social struggle.
  • 以上海之洋行为业 - Business in the maritime trade in Shanghai, indicating a specific profession and location.
  • 自置洋船五 - Owns five foreign ships, indicating a significant investment in maritime trade.
  • 在东西两洋贸易 - Conducts trade in both the East and West Oceans, indicating a broad trading network.
  • 每船必有标客以御盗贼 - Each ship must have a guard to protect against thieves, indicating a security measure.
  • 甲子春，船将开行，大宴标客 - Spring of the year 1844 (Jiashen year), the ship is about to set sail, and a grand banquet for the guards.
  • 招优演剧，甚盛设也 - Banquet included performances by entertainers and was very elaborate.
  • 标客自然首座，傲睨一切 - Guards naturally took the first seats and looked down on everything and everyone else.
  • 余舅命其子侄陪宴 - Uncle ordered his nephews and nieces to accompany him to the banquet.
  • 见客倨甚，切切私议，欲试其能 - Guards were very arrogant, whispered and discussed among themselves, and wanted to test their abilities.
  • 半酣小歇，肃客入园散步 - Taking a short break during the banquet, inviting the guests to take a walk in the garden.
  • 坚请试其技 - Insisted on testing their skills.
  • 客左右顾，见道旁有卧柳，曰：「此碍步，请为公子去之。」 - Guard looked around, saw a lying willow by the path, and said, 'This obstructs the way, please allow me to remove it for the young master.'
  • 迅以掌劈柳本，截然中断，如斧劈者 - Quickly chopped the base of the willow with his hand, cut cleanly in half, like it was cut with an axe.
  • 众皆咋舌 - Everyone was amazed and gasped.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 甲子春 - Refers to the spring of the year 1844, which is the Jiashen year in the Chinese zodiac.
  • 上海之洋行为业 - Indicates that the trade was conducted in Shanghai, a major port city during the Qing dynasty.
  • 洋船 - Refers to foreign ships, indicating international trade.
  • 标客 - Refers to guards or bodyguards, often hired to protect against piracy and theft.
  • 演剧 - Refers to theatrical performances, which were common entertainment during banquets.
  • 首座 - Refers to the most honored seat at a banquet.
  • 傲睨一切 - Describes a haughty and disdainful attitude.
  • 子侄 - Refers to nephews and nieces.
  • 切切私议 - Describes whispering and discussing among themselves.
  • 半酣小歇 - Describes taking a short break during a banquet.
  • 肃客入园散步 - Describes inviting guests to take a walk in the garden.
  • 坚请试其技 - Describes insisting on testing one's skills.
  • 迅以掌劈柳本，截然中断，如斧劈者 - Describes a dramatic and impressive display of physical strength.
  • 众皆咋舌 - Describes a reaction of amazement and surprise.

Proper Nouns

  • 余舅金氏 - The name of the narrator's maternal uncle.
  • 上海 - A city in China, known for its port and trade.
  • 甲子 - A term used in the Chinese calendar, referring to the year 1844.
  • 标客 - Guards or bodyguards.
  • 子侄 - Nephews and nieces.
  • 难女 - A woman facing challenges or difficulties.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 矫矫不群 - Classical phrase meaning 'exceedingly proud and independent,' describing someone who stands out from the crowd.
  • 募化 - Means 'to beg for alms' or 'to solicit donations,' often used in the context of religious or charitable activities.
  • 轻薄之伙 - Refers to a group of disrespectful or frivolous people, often used to describe those who lack respect or seriousness.
  • 疾趋 - Means 'to rush quickly' or 'to hurry,' often used to describe someone moving with great speed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淮阳难民 - Refers to refugees from Huaiyang, a region in ancient China, indicating a specific historical context of displacement and hardship.
  • 洋行 - Refers to a foreign trading company or a Western-style trading house, reflecting the presence of Western influence in China during the period.
  • 脚夫 - Refers to porters or laborers who carry goods, often used in the context of manual labor and transportation.
  • 糖包 - Refers to sugar packets, indicating the presence of trade and commerce involving sugar.

Proper Nouns

  • 淮阳 - The name of a region in ancient China, indicating the origin of the refugees.
  • 洋行 - The name of a foreign trading company, reflecting the presence of Western influence.
  • 糖包 - The name of a type of sugar packet, indicating the presence of trade and commerce involving sugar.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女大怒曰 - The woman became very angry and said.
  • 汝曹欺压孤女，使之内伤 - You all bullied the orphan girl, causing her internal injury.
  • 罪在不赦，非多给钱养伤，事不能已矣 - The crime is unforgivable, unless you pay more money for her medical treatment, the matter cannot be settled.
  • 时吆喝之声达于内，主人止戏，客亦出观 - The shouting reached inside, and the host stopped the play. The guests also came out to watch.
  • 攘臂而前，女以一掌拍客胸，跌去数丈，入柜内如菩萨座 - He advanced with his arms raised. The woman struck the guest's chest with one hand, and he fell back several feet, falling into the cabinet as if sitting on a Buddha's seat.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孤女 - Refers to an orphan girl, emphasizing her vulnerability.
  • 标客 - Refers to a bet or wager, suggesting a gambling context.
  • 菩萨座 - A metaphorical reference to a Buddha's seat, indicating a dramatic and exaggerated fall.
  • 海洋 - A metaphorical reference to the world, suggesting a vast and competitive environment.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日 - Means 'the next day' or 'the following day.'
  • 聞官以舟與資 - '聞官' means 'heard from the official,' and '以舟與資' means 'provided boats and funds.'
  • 護送难民出境 - '護送' means 'escort,' '难民' means 'refugees,' and '出境' means 'out of the country.'
  • 少年兄弟訪至馬頭 - '少年兄弟' means 'young brothers,' '訪至' means 'visited,' and '馬頭' means 'boat landing' or 'wharf.'
  • 挨舟觅女 - '挨舟' means 'searched through the boats,' and '觅女' means 'looked for the girl.'
  • 衣冠雖破，冠藍頂冠 - '衣冠' means 'clothes and hat,' '雖破' means 'though worn,' and '冠藍頂冠' means 'wearing a blue-topped hat.'
  • 方絮絮教训 - '方' means 'just,' '絮絮' means 'repeatedly,' and '教训' means 'teaching' or 'admonishing.'
  • 俯首垂泪 - '俯首' means 'bowed head,' and '垂泪' means 'tears streaming down.'
  • 極譽女能 - '極譽' means 'highly praised,' and '女能' means 'the girl's ability.'
  • 因何窘迫至是 - '因何' means 'why,' '窘迫' means 'in a difficult situation,' and '至是' means 'to this extent.'
  • 忝為都阃 - '忝為' means 'to humbly hold the position of,' and '都阃' refers to a military officer.
  • 以老致仕 - '以老' means 'due to old age,' and '致仕' means 'to retire.'
  • 不意今夏雨甚河决 - '不意' means 'unfortunately,' '今夏' means 'this summer,' '雨甚' means 'heavy rain,' and '河决' means 'river overflow.'
  • 田庐皆没 - '田庐' means 'fields and houses,' and '皆没' means 'all submerged.'
  • 不能不随众觅食 - '不能不' means 'could not help but,' '随众' means 'follow the crowd,' and '觅食' means 'seek food.'
  • 用泰山压顶势伤一标客 - '用泰山压顶势' means 'using the force of a falling mountain,' and '伤一标客' means 'injured a traveler.'
  • 女子何可逞强 - '女子何可' means 'how can a woman,' and '逞强' means 'show off strength.'
  • 擅动煞手 - '擅动' means 'arbitrarily use,' and '煞手' means 'deadly force.'
  • 败人衣食 - '败' means 'ruin,' and '衣食' means 'clothing and food.'
  • 亲家为渐军水师提督 - '亲家' means 'relative,' '为' means 'is,' '渐军' means 'Jian Army,' '水师提督' means 'naval commander.'
  • 婿亦开府矣 - '婿' means 'son-in-law,' '亦' means 'also,' '开府' means 'established his own office.'
  • 将送女完姻 - '将' means 'will,' '送女' means 'send the daughter,' and '完姻' means 'complete the marriage.'
  • 依以终身也 - '依' means 'rely on,' '以' means 'to,' and '终身' means 'for life.'
  • 诺诺而退 - '诺诺' means 'nodding affirmatively,' and '而退' means 'and withdrew.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女子之强者 - Refers to strong women, emphasizing their exceptional qualities.

  • 强欲留宿 - Means 'forcibly wanted to stay overnight,' showing his aggressive intentions.
  • 卖艺不卖身 - Means 'selling performances, not oneself,' a common phrase used by performers to maintain their dignity.
  • 犯我规耶 - Means 'is he breaking my rules?' expressing the class's defense of their code.
  • 拳击班主伏地 - Means 'punched the master of the troupe to the ground,' describing the violent act.
  • 良家妇女尚不敢抗 - Means 'even respectable women would not dare to resist,' emphasizing the martial arts champion's power.
  • 尔等既卖伎，何敢拂之，自取苦恼耶 - Means 'you all are selling your performances, so why do you dare to defy him, causing yourselves trouble?' a rhetorical question to dissuade the performers.
  • 迎武举而慰解之 - Means 'greeted the martial arts champion and tried to pacify him,' showing the performer's attempt to defuse the situation.
  • 官人果与妾有情 - Means 'if the official truly has feelings for me,' indicating the performer's willingness to engage if there is genuine interest.
  • 以夜持五十金来 - Means 'bring fifty taels of gold at night,' a specific demand for payment.
  • 否则，不能承也 - Means 'otherwise, I cannot comply,' indicating the conditions for her agreement.
  • 五十金非难事 - Means 'fifty taels of gold is not a difficult matter,' showing the performer's confidence in the martial arts champion's ability to pay.
  • 果处子亦不为费 - Means 'even if she is a virgin, it will not be a waste,' a dismissive comment on the performer's value.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 卖艺不卖身 - A common phrase used by performers to maintain their dignity and avoid compromising their virtue.
  • 五十金 - A significant amount of money, reflecting the economic context and the value placed on certain performances.
  • 处子 - A virgin, indicating the performer's status and the martial arts champion's dismissive attitude towards her.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This opening question and answer structure sets up the theme of women's exceptional abilities.
  • This complex sentence introduces the martial arts champion and his background, providing context for the conflict.
  • This clause describes the martial arts champion's aggressive intentions towards the performer.
  • This dialogue highlights the class's defense of their code and the martial arts champion's violation of it.
  • This action sequence shows the martial arts champion's violent reaction.
  • This collective response emphasizes the martial arts champion's power and the performers' fear.
  • This dialogue shows the performer's attempt to defuse the situation and set conditions for her agreement.
  • This final dialogue reveals the martial arts champion's dismissive attitude and the performer's value.

  • 寒苦，我命也 - Means 'poverty and hardship are my fate,' accepting one's circumstances.
  • 不能仰人鼻息 - Means 'I cannot live by breathing on others' noses,' expressing independence and self-reliance.
  • 还我故人 - Means 'return me to my old friends,' indicating a desire to reconnect with familiar people.
  • 任我所之 - Means 'let me go where I please,' expressing freedom of movement.
  • 夫人苦留之，不可 - Means 'the wife tried hard to keep him, but he would not,' showing persistence and refusal.
  • 竞脱华服，衣故衣 - Means 'he finally took off his fine clothes and put on his old clothes,' indicating a change in appearance.
  • 不名一钱 - Means 'not having a single coin,' emphasizing extreme poverty.
  • 携来时笔砚而去 - Means 'taking with him the brush and inkstone he brought,' indicating a return to a simpler life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 异乡人 - Refers to someone who is not from the local area, often used in contexts of travel or migration.
  • 曹邱 - A term for a matchmaker or go-between, often used in the context of arranging marriages.
  • 主人 - Refers to a host or the person in charge of a household, showing respect and hospitality.
  • 赘婿 - Refers to a son-in-law who moves into his wife's family home, often with the expectation of supporting the family financially.
  • 寒苦 - Refers to poverty and hardship, a common theme in classical Chinese literature and social commentary.
  • 仰人鼻息 - A metaphor for living by the mercy or favor of others, often used to describe a lack of independence.
  • 故人 - Refers to old friends or acquaintances, often used in the context of nostalgia or a return to familiar surroundings.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '某异乡人，此间无人相识，谁作曹邱者' introduces the speaker's situation and the need for a matchmaker, requiring careful reading to understand the context.
  • The rhetorical question and offer of hospitality '先生果有意乎？我即主人，请以训我子何如' must be read together to understand the speaker's intent.
  • The brief and direct response '公欣诺' must be understood in the context of the preceding dialogue.
  • The sequence '具衣冠什物延公，使二幼子受业' describes the preparations for the speaker's arrival and the arrangement for his children's education, requiring careful parsing.
  • The long sequence '阅半载，见其性情通达，学问书法俱佳，遂遣媒纳为赘婿' marks the passage of time, the speaker's positive qualities, and the formal arrangement of his marriage, requiring careful reading to understand the progression.
  • The sequence '未几商卒，长子袭父业，渺视公而戏弄之，且不使诸弟入学' describes the death of the merchant, the inheritance of his business, and the disrespectful behavior of the eldest son, requiring careful parsing to understand the sequence of events.
  • The sequence '读闺中，其夫人讥之曰' introduces a private setting and the wife's criticism, requiring careful reading to understand the context.
  • The sequence '丈夫不能身自振发，而依亲戚为事，妾亦何颜' expresses the wife's sense of shame and helplessness, requiring careful parsing to understand the emotional state.
  • The sequence '公怒欲去，夫人曰' describes the speaker's anger and the wife's response, requiring careful reading to understand the interaction.
  • The sequence '妾奉严命，相随终身，岂有他志？然不得不劝夫子自立也' expresses the wife's commitment and advice, requiring careful parsing to understand the balance of loyalty and concern.
  • The sequence '今夫子舍妾而去，将焉往' introduces a hypothetical scenario and the wife's concern, requiring careful reading to understand the emotional context.
  • The sequence '寒苦，我命也，不能仰人鼻息' expresses the speaker's acceptance of his circumstances and his desire for independence, requiring careful parsing to understand the emotional state.
  • The sequence '还我故人，任我所之' expresses the speaker's desire to return to his old friends and his freedom of movement, requiring careful reading to understand the emotional context.
  • The sequence '夫人苦留之，不可，竞脱华服，衣故衣，不名一钱，携来时笔砚而去' describes the wife's attempts to keep the speaker, his final decision to leave, and his return to a simpler life, requiring careful parsing to understand the sequence of events.

  • 得以考终焉 - Means 'and was able to die peacefully,' indicating a peaceful end.
  • 芗厈曰 - Means 'Xiang Yan said,' introducing a commentary.
  • 仙人何播弄下愚 - Means 'why does the immortal play with the foolish,' indicating a question about the role of immortals.
  • 益之适以损之 - Means 'to benefit him actually harms him,' indicating a paradoxical situation.
  • 使我遇之，当求性命双修之道 - Means 'if I met him, I would seek the way of cultivating both life and spirit,' indicating a philosophical approach.
  • 则超凡入圣可期矣 - Means 'then it is possible to transcend the ordinary and become a sage,' indicating a high spiritual goal.
  • 何仅以温饱自得哉 - Means 'why just satisfy oneself with warmth and food,' indicating a criticism of a low aim.
  • 或曰 - Means 'someone said,' introducing a counter-argument.
  • 君所以不得遇仙人，贪故也 - Means 'you did not meet the immortal because of greed,' indicating a reason for not meeting the immortal.
  • 竟无词以对 - Means 'finally had no words to respond,' indicating a lack of a satisfactory reply.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • This sentence introduces a short period of time and a significant loss caused by Zhang Zi.
  • This sentence introduces a collective decision or plan by the gamblers.
  • This sentence expresses surprise and asks why Zhang Zi has become invincible.
  • This sentence emphasizes the urgency to understand the cause of Zhang Zi's success.
  • This sentence introduces the discovery of reliable information about Mr. Li and the gamblers' decision to deal with him.
  • This sentence describes the plan to bribe beggars and target Mr. Li.
  • This sentence describes the execution of the plan and the violent act.
  • This sentence describes the legal consequences of the act.
  • This sentence describes the consequence of the act and the regret of Mr. Li.
  • This sentence describes the positive outcome for Zhang Zi and his gesture of gratitude to Mr. Li.
  • This sentence introduces a commentary on the role of immortals.

  • This sentence introduces a counter-argument and a lack of a satisfactory reply.

  • 有楚生某 - A phrase meaning 'There was a man named Chu from a certain place.'

  • 邻有寡妇 - A statement meaning 'There was a widow living next door.'
  • 与外村少年狎 - A statement meaning 'She had an intimate relationship with a young man from another village.'
  • 少年过寡妇家，偶见生妇，极为垂涎 - A statement meaning 'The young man visited the widow's house and happened to see Chu's wife, who greatly aroused his desire.'
  • 以重赀贻寡妇，谋通之 - A statement meaning 'He gave the widow a large sum of money and plotted to seduce her.'
  • 彼宴尔新婚，夫妻和睦 - A statement meaning 'He is newly married and his marriage is harmonious.'
  • 且良家妇，不可以势逼，亦不能以利诱 - A statement meaning 'Moreover, she is a good family woman and cannot be forced by power or enticed by money.'
  • 虽其夫毕姻后，出外教读，但有老媪作伴，亦难以游词进 - A statement meaning 'Even though his husband has gone out to teach after the wedding, and there is only an old woman for company, it is still difficult to approach her with flattery.'
  • 实无法可施也 - A statement meaning 'In fact, there is no way to do it.'
  • 少年情不自禁，以重价觅得积贼闷香来 - A statement meaning 'The young man could not control his feelings and found a large amount of opium from a notorious thief.'
  • 我已得计，第不能穿穴逾墙 - A statement meaning 'I have a plan, but I cannot break through walls or climb over fences.'
  • 若诱至汝家，事必偕矣 - A statement meaning 'If I can lure her to your house, the matter will surely be successful.'
  • 是不难 - A statement meaning 'This is not difficult.'
  • 使少年入市，市食物之精者，往候生妇，以甘言结好 - A statement meaning 'Have the young man go to the market, buy the finest food, and visit Chu's wife, using sweet words to win her favor.'
  • 后此屡往，密甚 - A statement meaning 'After this, he visited frequently, becoming very close.'
  • 生妇知寡妇家无男子，且近邻，以礼往答 - A statement meaning 'Chu's wife knew that the widow's house had no men and was a close neighbor, so she went to visit her out of courtesy.'
  • 寡妇留生妇入卧室，饭之，而遣媪妇守门户 - A statement meaning 'The widow invited Chu's wife into her bedroom, served her a meal, and sent the old woman to guard the door.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阎王 - Refers to King Yama, the god of the underworld in Chinese mythology.
  • 十六官 - Refers to the sixteen officials in the underworld, a concept from Chinese folklore.
  • 聪明正直之为神 - A belief that the wise and upright are considered gods in Chinese culture.
  • 阴曹 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead.
  • 阳间 - Refers to the mortal world or the realm of the living.
  • 显官 - Refers to high-ranking officials in the government.
  • 正人君子 - Refers to upright and virtuous men.
  • 积贼闷香 - Refers to opium, a drug that was often associated with criminal activities in historical contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 夫何以生人署阎王之职，而又不使之主政 - This rhetorical question introduces a paradoxical situation where a living person is appointed as King Yama but not allowed to govern.
  • 十六官皆可代也，何用生为 - This statement emphasizes the redundancy of having a living person in such a position.
  • 且聪明正直之为神，此生与余一人，交未能信，且为正人君子，岂阴曹之鬼，犹不如生耶 - This complex sentence contrasts the moral standing of the living and the dead, questioning the value of being alive.
  • 或曰：「阳间显官，岂尽正人君子为之，君何泥也？」 - This rhetorical question challenges the assumption that all high-ranking officials are virtuous.
  • 余笑曰：「诺。」 - This brief response indicates agreement or acquiescence.

Proper Nouns

  • 阎王 - King Yama, the god of the underworld.
  • 十六官 - The sixteen officials in the underworld.
  • 楚生某 - A man named Chu from a certain place.
  • 寡妇 - A widow.
  • 少年 - A young man.
  • 积贼闷香 - Opium, a drug.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 时少年预伏室中 - At that time, a young man was hiding in the room. '预伏' implies a premeditated or hidden presence.
  • 室焚闷香 - The room was filled with a suffocating incense. '闷香' suggests a heavy, oppressive scent.
  • 妇觉香甚软 - The woman felt the incense was very soft. '软' could metaphorically imply a lack of resistance or a seductive quality.
  • 伏于座 - She lay down on the seat. '伏' means to lie down or crouch.
  • 少年突出 - The young man suddenly appeared. '突出' implies a sudden and unexpected emergence.
  • 拥妇登床 - He dragged the woman onto the bed. '拥' means to drag or pull.
  • 寡妇避去 - The widow fled away. '避去' means to flee or escape.
  • 妇手足麻木 - The woman's limbs were numb. '麻木' indicates a loss of sensation or paralysis.
  • 口不能言 - She could not speak. This phrase emphasizes her inability to communicate.
  • 而心甚了了 - But her mind was very clear. '了了' means clear or lucid.
  • 任其淫狎 - She allowed him to indulge in his lust. '淫狎' refers to lewd or lascivious behavior.
  • 饮泣而已 - She could only weep. '饮泣' means to cry or sob.
  • 少年思得其欢心 - The young man wanted to please her. '欢心' means to please or delight.
  • 许与衣饰 - He promised to give her clothes and ornaments. '衣饰' refers to clothing and jewelry.
  • 妇益忿怒 - The woman became even more angry. '忿怒' means anger or rage.
  • 泪如泉涌 - Tears flowed like a spring. This is a vivid metaphor for excessive weeping.
  • 少年知难久留 - The young man knew he could not stay long. '难久留' implies that he had to leave soon.
  • 草草讫事而逸 - He hastily finished and fled. '草草' means hastily or carelessly, and '逸' means to flee.
  • 妇醒，索寡妇 - The woman woke up and searched for the widow. '索' means to search or seek.
  • 不见，破其室中什物而归 - She did not find her, so she destroyed the belongings in the room and returned. '破' means to destroy, and '什物' refers to household items.
  • 郁郁成疾 - She became depressed and fell ill. '郁郁' means depression or melancholy.
  • 其父，名讼师也 - Her father was a famous lawyer. '讼师' refers to a lawyer or litigator.
  • 往问之 - He went to ask her. '问' means to inquire or ask.
  • 女欲求计划 - The daughter wanted a plan. '计划' means a plan or scheme.
  • 以实情告 - She told him the truth. '实情' means the truth or reality.
  • 父责其不应轻出 - The father blamed her for not being careful. '轻出' means to act recklessly.
  • 然彼以阴谋来，我当以诡计报之 - But since he came with deceit, I will repay him with cunning. '阴谋' means a secret plot, and '诡计' means a cunning scheme.
  • 随作一函，纳女枕畔曰 - He then wrote a letter and placed it beside her pillow, saying... '函' means a letter or document.
  • 能从之，则使媪来，我命两兄助之 - If you agree, I will send the old woman to come, and I will have my two brothers help her. '从之' means to agree, and '媪' refers to an old woman or servant.
  • 父去，妇阅其书，大喜，病寻愈 - After the father left, the woman read the letter, was greatly pleased, and soon recovered from her illness. '寻' means soon or shortly after.
  • 益自修饰 - She began to dress and groom herself more carefully. '修饰' means to dress or adorn.
  • 使媪强寡妇来谢过 - She sent the old woman to force the widow to come and apologize. '强' means to force or compel.
  • 与亲昵之 - She befriended her closely. '亲昵' means to be close or intimate.
  • 留寡妇宿 - She kept the widow overnight. '宿' means to stay or lodge.
  • 密告之曰 - She secretly told her, saying... '密告' means to secretly inform or confide.
  • 前日之事，曷不先与我谋，是所恨也 - Why didn't you consult me about the matter from the day before? That is what I regret. '曷' means why, and '所恨' means what is regretted.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 寡妇 - Refers to a widow, a woman whose husband has died.
  • 讼师 - Refers to a lawyer or litigator, a professional who handles legal disputes.
  • 阴谋 - Refers to a secret plot or scheme, often with a negative connotation.
  • 诡计 - Refers to a cunning or deceptive plan.
  • 媪 - Refers to an old woman or servant, often used in a respectful or formal context.
  • 两兄 - Refers to the father's two brothers, indicating a familial connection and support.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 第事巳如此 - A phrase indicating that the situation has already reached a certain state, suggesting resignation or inevitability.
  • 縱決西江之水，難洗清白 - A metaphorical expression meaning that even if one were to unleash the waters of the West River, it would be difficult to cleanse oneself of guilt or suspicion.
  • 良人久客不归 - A common phrase in Classical Chinese literature referring to a husband who has been away for a long time and has not returned.
  • 晨昏孤宿 - Refers to the loneliness experienced during both morning and evening, emphasizing the widow's isolation.
  • 乃子实获我心 - A phrase indicating that the young man truly understands the widow's feelings.
  • 面许我衣饰，何食言也 - A phrase questioning why the young man would break his promise regarding the clothing and ornaments.
  • 是人富而多情 - Describes the young man as wealthy and emotionally expressive.
  • 实慕娘子，无路可通 - Indicates that the young man genuinely admires the widow but lacks a way to express it.
  • 非负所许也 - Denies that the young man is breaking his promise.
  • 我为导引何如 - A question asking if the widow should guide the young man.
  • 须防外人耳目 - A caution to be wary of being seen or heard by outsiders.
  • 来必夜深人静 - Specifies that the young man will come when it is late at night and quiet.
  • 我逐媪候门，方无他虑 - Indicates that the widow will wait at the door to avoid other concerns.
  • 因与订期而去 - Means that they agreed on a time and the widow left.
  • 归告少年，自诩有功 - The widow returns and boasts about her success to the young man.
  • 急购鲜衣美珠 - Describes the young man's immediate purchase of fine clothes and beautiful jewels.
  • 使寡妇先致之 - Means that the young man sends the widow to deliver these items first.
  • 如期留寡妇家 - Indicates that the young man arrived at the agreed time at the widow's house.
  • 漏四下，潜以指叩门 - Describes the young man arriving at midnight and silently knocking on the door.
  • 与妇携手入室，即欲乱之 - Describes the young man taking the widow's hand and entering the room, immediately intending to have relations with her.
  • 请同酌三杯以助兴 - The widow asks the young man to drink three cups together to enhance the mood.
  • 案上酒餐具奋 - Describes the table set with wine and utensils.
  • 妇酌酒以进，少年目视而不饮 - The widow pours the wine and offers it to the young man, who looks at it but does not drink.
  • 妇举杯坐怀中，口含以哺 - The widow holds the cup to the young man's mouth and feeds him the wine.
  • 少年迷乱，接而吞之 - The young man becomes confused and accepts the wine, drinking it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西江 - Refers to the West River, a significant river in Chinese geography.
  • 良人 - A term used to refer to one's husband, often in a respectful manner.
  • 晨昏 - Refers to the times of day, specifically morning and evening, often used to describe the routine of daily life.
  • 面许 - A promise made face-to-face, indicating a personal commitment.
  • 食言 - Breaking a promise or saying something one does not intend to do.
  • 导引 - Guidance or leading someone, often in a secretive or covert manner.
  • 耳目 - Refers to the senses of hearing and seeing, often used to describe being observed or noticed.
  • 漏四下 - Midnight, a time when it is very late and quiet.
  • 潜以指叩门 - Silently knocking on the door, indicating stealth and secrecy.
  • 乱之 - Having relations, often used in a euphemistic manner.

Proper Nouns

  • 寡妇 - The widow.
  • 少年 - The young man.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外客聞內宅慘呼 - 外客 refers to guests outside the house, and 內宅 refers to the inner quarters of the house. 慘呼 means a loud, distressing cry.
  • 新郎衣履如故 - 衣履如故 means the groom's clothes and shoes were unchanged, implying he appeared normal on the surface.
  • 狂躍而出 - 狂躍 means to jump wildly or frantically, and 而出 means to go out.
  • 群欲詢之 - 群 means the group, and 欲詢之 means they wanted to ask him.
  • 已疾奔出外 - 疾奔 means to run quickly, and 出外 means to go outside.
  • 客皆追行 - 客 means the guests, and 追行 means to follow him.
  • 里許 - 里 is a unit of distance, and 許 means about. Together, it means 'about a mile.'
  • 遇大河 - 遇 means to encounter, and 大河 means a large river.
  • 即躍入水而沒 - 即 means immediately, 躍入水 means to jump into the water, and 而沒 means to disappear or sink.
  • 客呼漁舟捞救 - 呼 means to call, 漁舟 means fishing boat, and 捞救 means to rescue.

  • 挂面 - Noodles, often used as a gift during celebrations.
  • 筋烛 - Strong candles, also used as gifts.
  • 甄别 - To distinguish or evaluate, often used in the context of removing officials from office.
  • 囹圄 - Refers to prisons or jails.
  • 春风化雨 - A metaphor for benevolent governance, where the ruler's policies are like gentle rain and warm breezes that nurture the people.

  • 筋烛 - Strong candles.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公呼使前 - Formal expression meaning 'the official called the person forward.'

  • 见汝面有凶横气 - Means 'I saw that you had a fierce and violent appearance.'
  • 历历开导 - Means 'I patiently and clearly instructed you.'
  • 汝色渐和 - Means 'your expression gradually became more peaceful.'
  • 逾今三载 - Means 'after three years.'

  • 其为不改凶心，致有此事 - Means 'it seems you did not change your violent nature, leading to this incident.'

  • 与其受尽责扑而后陈，不如直陈无隐，身不受亏，而恩犹可冀 - Means 'it is better to confess everything openly and honestly, so that you do not suffer more punishment, and there is still hope for mercy.'
  • 洞见小人肺腑矣 - Means 'you have seen through my heart.'
  • 昔年我与被杀之某，共交一嫠妇 - Means 'years ago, I had a relationship with the woman who was killed.'
  • 后某独占而屏我 - Means 'later, he took her exclusively and excluded me.'
  • 是不平 - Means 'this was unfair.'
  • 嗣公至以善劝人，人皆革面洗心 - Means 'after the official came and taught people to be good, everyone changed their ways.'
  • 其不能者，为众所不齿 - Means 'those who could not change were despised by others.'
  • 某偕妇出亡 - Means 'I left with the woman.'
  • 我亦闻公言自悔 - Means 'I also heard the official's words and repented.'
  • 今公甫谢事 - Means 'now the official has just retired.'
  • 某与妇仍归旧村 - Means 'I and the woman returned to our old village.'
  • 且讪笑村众曰 - Means 'and we mocked the villagers, saying...'
  • 汝等不过畏陆青天耳 - Means 'you are only afraid of the upright official.'
  • 渠不能自保，今尚有此不近人情官耶 - Means 'he cannot protect himself, and now there is still such an inhuman official?'
  • 小人恨其词，触前忿，潜杀之 - Means 'I hated his words, and my anger was stirred, so I secretly killed him.'
  • 意谓无人能破此寨，不意公竟先觉也 - Means 'I thought no one could break this conspiracy, but I did not expect the official to discover it.'
  • 虽死奚辞 - Means 'even if I die, I have no regrets.'
  • 后官怜其尚有良心，薄其罪而流之 - Means 'the official felt pity for me because I still had a conscience, so he reduced my punishment and exiled me.'
  • 国法，凡甄别官，例引见 - Means 'according to the national law, officials who are being evaluated are usually summoned for an audience.'

  • 见其岸然道貌 - Means 'he saw the official's dignified and virtuous appearance.'
  • 奏对从容不苟 - Means 'the official answered questions calmly and without carelessness.'
  • 圣祖大悦 - Means 'the Emperor Kangxi was very pleased.'
  • 卿相复保奏之 - Means 'the grand councilors and high officials recommended him again.'
  • 用为礼部主事，洊升侍御，致仕归 - Means 'he was appointed as an official in the Ministry of Rites, then promoted to a higher position, and eventually retired.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乡约 - Refers to village regulations or community rules, often used in traditional Chinese rural governance.
  • 陆青天 - A colloquial term for an upright and fair official, often used in historical contexts.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人之所以异于禽兽者，廉耻二字 - Philosophical statement meaning 'The difference between humans and animals lies in the two concepts of integrity and shame.'
  • 若忘此，虽圣贤不能化也 - Means 'If one forgets this, even sages cannot transform them.'
  • 盗贼之三犯者，只好饮之以醋而已 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'For a thief who has committed three crimes, one can only make them drink vinegar.'
  • 杀人者，虽因奸也，实缘讪笑公而顿激忿怒 - Means 'A murderer, though provoked by jealousy, is actually driven to sudden anger by mocking him.'
  • 甄别公者，直斯人不若矣，当以醋灌之 - Means 'Those who distinguish themselves by mocking him are even worse than him, and should be made to drink vinegar.'
  • 补骗子(五则) - Title meaning 'Supplement on Fraudsters (Five Cases).'
  • 京师布肆，大业也 - Means 'In the capital, a cloth shop is a major business.'
  • 拣既，谓肆主曰 - Means 'After selecting, he said to the shop owner.'
  • 我初学肩贩，须同伙来定，姑待之可乎 - Means 'I am new to carrying goods, and need my partner to come to finalize, can I wait a bit?'
  • 见买卖渐稀，谓肆主曰 - Means 'Seeing that business was slowing down, he said to the shop owner.'
  • 我尚未饭，伙又不来，身无余钱，所存赀本银两系合伙者，未便换钱，腹中馁甚，奈何 - Means 'I haven't had my meal yet, my partner hasn't come, I have no extra money, the silver I have is from my partnership, so I can't exchange it, I'm very hungry, what should I do?'
  • 既有银两，无患无钱，若未便换，典之可也 - Means 'Since you have silver, you don't have to worry about lacking money, if you can't exchange it, you can pawn it.'
  • 乡人欣然出银，灿然一提，揖肆主曰 - Means 'The villager happily took out the silver, presented it in a shining manner, and bowed to the shop owner.'
  • 我不知质库在何处，此银二十两，请烦贵伙代为入典 - Means 'I don't know where the pawnshop is, this silver is twenty taels, please ask your partner to deposit it for me.'
  • 吾侪小人，果腹而已，不求甚佳 - Means 'We common folk, just want to fill our stomachs, we don't demand much.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 人之所以异于禽兽者，廉耻二字 - Refers to the Confucian moral principle that distinguishes humans from animals.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city, historically the political and cultural center of China.
  • 布肆 - Refers to a cloth shop, a common business in traditional Chinese society.
  • 肩贩 - Refers to a peddler who carries goods on their shoulders, a common occupation in traditional Chinese society.
  • 质库 - Refers to a pawnshop, a financial institution in traditional Chinese society.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase introduces a philosophical contrast between humans and animals.
  • This concessive clause emphasizes the importance of integrity and shame.
  • This metaphorical statement uses vinegar as a symbolic punishment.
  • This complex sentence explains the motive behind a murder.
  • This final statement uses vinegar as a symbolic punishment for those who mock others.
  • This title introduces a series of cases about fraudsters.
  • This phrase introduces the setting of a major business in the capital.
  • This phrase introduces the villager's request to the shop owner.
  • This phrase explains the villager's need for his partner.
  • This phrase explains the villager's situation as business slows down.
  • This long sentence explains the villager's predicament.
  • This clause offers a solution to the villager's problem.
  • This phrase describes the villager's action and demeanor.
  • This phrase explains the villager's request to the shop owner.
  • This phrase explains the villager's modest aspirations.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 余钱八百余文 - Leftover money; eight hundred plus cash coins.
  • 掷柜上 - Tossed onto the counter.
  • 坐守至晚 - Sat waiting until evening.
  • 其伙竟不来 - His partner finally didn't come.
  • 乡人惶然谓肆主曰 - The villagers anxiously said to the shop owner.

  • 盍以我二十金之券，抵运布去 - Why not use my twenty gold coins' voucher to pay for the shipment of cloth?
  • 尚有余钱，明日偕伙来算可乎 - There is still some leftover money, can we settle the rest tomorrow with my partner?
  • 应曰 - Replied.
  • 可 - Yes.
  • 郑重出券 - Carefully presented the voucher.

  • 雇驴车 - Hired a donkey cart.

  • 候之三日 - Waited for three days.
  • 竟不来 - Finally didn't come.
  • 乃赴质库取银 - Then went to the pawnshop to get the silver.
  • 仅有二两 - Only had two taels.
  • 我所质者二十两大锭也 - The twenty taels' large ingots I pledged.
  • 何以仅止此 - Why is it only this much?
  • 典主查簿示之曰 - The pawnbroker checked the ledger and showed it saying.
  • 是日贵伙以二十金质钱一串 - On that day, your partner pledged twenty gold coins for a string of cash coins.
  • 我疑其所需过少 - I suspected that what they needed was too little.

  • 曰 - Said.
  • 是客物也，只需此为饭食耳 - These are the guest's things, they only needed this for food.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 余钱 - Refers to leftover money, a common concept in ancient Chinese economic transactions.
  • 文 - Ancient Chinese currency unit, equivalent to copper coins.
  • 柜 - Counter or desk, commonly used in shops and markets.
  • 乡人 - Villagers or local people, often used to refer to ordinary citizens.
  • 肆主 - Shop owner, a common figure in ancient Chinese marketplaces.
  • 质库 - Pawnshop, a place where people could pledge items for cash.
  • 典主 - Pawnbroker, the person in charge of the pawnshop.
  • 质 - To pledge or pawn, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 券 - Voucher or receipt, used in transactions.
  • 银 - Silver, a valuable currency in ancient China.
  • 锭 - Ingots, a form of precious metal used as currency.
  • 利 - Interest, a concept used in financial transactions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 余钱八百余文，掷柜上，坐守至晚，其伙竟不来 - This sequence describes a series of actions: having leftover money, tossing it onto the counter, sitting and waiting until evening, and the partner finally not coming.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 與我一气 - Literally 'be in harmony with me,' meaning to be united or aligned with the principal wife.
  • 何事不可也 - Literally 'what cannot be done,' meaning anything is possible if they work together.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花婆 - A term for a matchmaker or procuress, often associated with courtesans and mistresses, reflecting the social practices of arranging marriages or relationships.
  • 正夫人 - Refers to the principal wife in a polygamous household, who holds a higher status than concubines.

  • 長跪 - A gesture of deep respect and submission, often used in formal or pleading situations.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有徐大诳者，丝市中伙也 - Describes Xu Da Kuang as a common figure in the silk market, where '伙' implies he is a typical or well-known person in that setting.
  • 喜诳人取笑 - Literally means 'likes to deceive people to make them laugh,' indicating a playful or mischievous nature.
  • 得其美称 - Means 'gains a good reputation,' suggesting that his deceptive behavior is seen as amusing rather than harmful.
  • 随居停至吴下贸易 - '居停' refers to a host or landlord, and '吴下' is a poetic term for the southern region of Wu (modern-day Jiangsu and Zhejiang). This phrase means 'traveled to the Wu region with his host for trade.'
  • 舟泊太子码头 - '太子码头' is a specific dock, likely named after a prince or a royal figure, indicating a place of significance.
  • 予起货去，汝留此须慎防之 - The host tells Xu to be cautious while he is away, as '起货去' means 'to go to fetch goods.'
  • 此处多骗子，勿为所诳 - Warns Xu that there are many deceivers in this place, and he should not be deceived himself.
  • 吾不诳人，人其诳我乎？ - Xu confidently asserts that he does not deceive others, implying he does not expect to be deceived in return.
  • 人果行其诳，我将诳谁矣 - Xu rhetorically asks who would deceive him if he does not deceive others, suggesting a reciprocal principle.
  • 咍！此处骗子，皆鲜衣盛服，充衣冠中人 - '咍' is an interjection expressing surprise or disapproval. Xu is surprised to learn that the deceivers are well-dressed and appear respectable.
  • 汝若遇之，必逢迎不惶，恐小巫见大巫，堕其术中而不觉也 - '逢迎不惶' means 'will be too eager to please,' and '小巫见大巫' is a metaphor for being outmatched by a superior. Xu is warned that he might be fooled by these deceivers.
  • 请予洋银二牧，我将作虎邱之游 - Xu asks for two silver dollars to go on a trip to Tiger Hill, a famous scenic spot.
  • 见肆有锡洋钱，为孩童所玩弄者，与真无异 - Xu notices counterfeit silver dollars being played with by children, which are indistinguishable from real ones.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 徐大诳 - Xu Da Kuang is a fictional or semi-fictional character, likely a commoner known for his playful deception.
  • 丝市 - Refers to the silk market, a bustling commercial area in ancient China.
  • 吴下 - A poetic term for the Wu region, known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 太子码头 - A specific dock, possibly named after a prince or a royal figure, indicating a place of importance.
  • 洋银 - Refers to foreign silver coins, likely introduced during the late Qing period.
  • 虎邱 - Tiger Hill, a famous scenic spot in Suzhou, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 锡洋钱 - Counterfeit silver coins, often made of tin or other metals, which were common in ancient China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 問其價 - Literally means 'ask for its price,' a common phrase used in transactions.
  • 枚值五六文 - Means 'each costs five or six coins,' indicating the price per item.
  • 徐置百枚 - '徐' means 'slowly' or 'gradually,' and '置' means 'to place' or 'to buy.' Together, it suggests buying a hundred items gradually.
  • 又以百余钱买一布囊 - '又以' means 'and with,' and '百余钱' means 'over a hundred coins.' This phrase indicates purchasing a cloth bag with additional money.
  • 以棉纸裹锡钱，作两封，纳囊中 - '以' means 'with,' '棉纸' means 'cotton paper,' '裹' means 'wrap,' '锡钱' means 'tinsel coins,' '作两封' means 'make two packets,' and '纳囊中' means 'put into the bag.' This describes wrapping the tinsel coins in cotton paper and placing them in the bag.
  • 负之归舟 - '负' means 'carry,' and '归舟' means 'back to the boat.' This phrase indicates carrying the bag back to the boat.
  • 易短褐毡冠，作乡人状 - '易' means 'change,' '短褐' means 'short coat,' '毡冠' means 'felt hat,' and '作乡人状' means 'pretend to be a villager.' This describes changing into simple rural attire.
  • 以红纸开绸缎账单，怀之 - '以' means 'with,' '红纸' means 'red paper,' '开' means 'write,' '绸缎账单' means 'silk fabric account,' and '怀之' means 'keep it in one's pocket.' This describes writing an account on red paper and keeping it in one's pocket.
  • 持雨盖负囊，游于市 - '持' means 'hold,' '雨盖' means 'umbrella,' '负' means 'carry,' '囊' means 'bag,' and '游于市' means 'wander in the market.' This describes holding an umbrella and carrying the bag while wandering in the market.
  • 见古玩铺前，坐一客，衣冠甚丽 - '见' means 'see,' '古玩铺' means 'antique shop,' '坐一客' means 'a seated customer,' and '衣冠甚丽' means 'dressed very elegantly.' This describes seeing a well-dressed customer sitting in front of an antique shop.
  • 时正隆冬，披火狐裘，貉冠皮鞋，手持玉嘴银头乌木烟管 - '时正隆冬' means 'it was the height of winter,' '披火狐裘' means 'wearing a fur coat of red fox,' '貉冠皮鞋' means 'wearing a hat and leather shoes of raccoon,' and '手持玉嘴银头乌木烟管' means 'holding a tobacco pipe with a jade mouthpiece, silver head, and ebony body.' This describes the customer's luxurious winter attire and accessories.
  • 徐有触于怀，乃坐对门巨室阶上 - '徐有触于怀' means 'gradually, something stirred in his heart,' and '乃坐对门巨室阶上' means 'then he sat on the steps of a large house across the street.' This describes the character's emotional state and action.
  • 出其单与锡洋布目前 - '出其单' means 'show his account,' '锡洋' means 'tinsel coins,' and '布目前' means 'in front of him.' This describes showing the account and the tinsel coins to the customer.
  • 以真洋钱颠掷 - '以' means 'with,' '真洋钱' means 'real foreign coins,' and '颠掷' means 'juggle.' This describes juggling real foreign coins.
  • 而口作怨词 - '而口作怨词' means 'and mutter complaints.' This describes the character muttering complaints.
  • 正自嗫嚅间，客见其多金，洋洋来前 - '正自嗫嚅间' means 'while he was muttering,' '客见其多金' means 'the customer saw he had a lot of money,' and '洋洋来前' means 'approached him arrogantly.' This describes the customer's reaction to the character's wealth.
  • 喝问曰：「汝何等人？坐我府前，意欲何为？」 - '喝问' means 'shout and ask,' '汝何等人' means 'who are you,' '坐我府前' means 'sitting in front of my house,' and '意欲何为' means 'what do you intend to do.' This describes the customer's aggressive questioning.
  • 徐忙收洋钱入囊，执账单拜曰 - '徐忙' means 'quickly,' '收洋钱入囊' means 'put the foreign coins back in the bag,' '执账单' means 'hold the account,' and '拜曰' means 'bow and say.' This describes the character's quick response and bowing.
  • 不识尊府，动扰不当 - '不识尊府' means 'do not recognize your esteemed residence,' and '动扰不当' means 'it is inappropriate to disturb you.' This describes the character's apology.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 火狐裘 - A luxurious fur coat made from red fox fur, symbolizing wealth and status.

  • 真洋钱 - Refers to real foreign coins, which were highly valued and often used in transactions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 徐置百枚，又以百余钱买一布囊 - This phrase can be read as two separate actions: buying a hundred items gradually and then purchasing a cloth bag with additional money.
  • 复大书曰「某年月日某乡某人置」 - The inscription inside the quotation marks is a specific date, place, and person, which must be clearly translated.
  • 以棉纸裹锡钱，作两封，纳囊中 - This phrase describes a series of actions: wrapping the tinsel coins in cotton paper, making two packets, and putting them in the bag.
  • 负之归舟，易短褐毡冠，作乡人状 - These actions describe carrying the bag back to the boat and changing into simple rural attire.
  • 以红纸开绸缎账单，怀之，持雨盖负囊，游于市 - This phrase describes writing an account on red paper, keeping it in one's pocket, holding an umbrella, carrying the bag, and wandering in the market.
  • 见古玩铺前，坐一客，衣冠甚丽 - This phrase describes seeing a well-dressed customer sitting in front of an antique shop.
  • 时正隆冬，披火狐裘，貉冠皮鞋，手持玉嘴银头乌木烟管 - This phrase describes the customer's luxurious winter attire and accessories.
  • 徐有触于怀，乃坐对门巨室阶上 - This phrase describes the character's emotional state and action.
  • 出其单与锡洋布目前，以真洋钱颠掷，而口作怨词 - This phrase describes showing the account and the tinsel coins, juggling real foreign coins, and muttering complaints.
  • 正自嗫嚅间，客见其多金，洋洋来前，喝问曰：「汝何等人？坐我府前，意欲何为？」 - This phrase describes the customer's aggressive questioning.
  • 徐忙收洋钱入囊，执账单拜曰：「不识尊府，动扰不当。」 - This phrase describes the character's quick response and apology.

Describes the aftermath of the case.

  • 刑书以八子是月日夜方在此行窃丝肆，岂能远往三百里外从盗乎？其为仇攀可知 - Complex sentence meaning 'the county magistrate argued that Huang Baizi was in the city that night, robbing a silk shop, so how could he have gone three hundred miles away to join in the theft? It is clear that this is a case of someone seeking revenge and falsely accusing him.' Describes the magistrate's defense of the speaker.

  • The sentence '供及黄八子，行文到县，刑书以八子是月日夜方在此行窃丝肆，岂能远往三百里外从盗乎？其为仇攀可知' must be read as a sequence of clauses that describe the involvement of the speaker and the magistrate's defense.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 純陽為仙，純陰為鬼 - Classical binary opposition where 'pure yang' symbolizes immortality or divinity, and 'pure yin' represents death or the underworld, reflecting traditional Chinese cosmology and dualism.
  • 刻求誠明 - '刻求' means 'to strive earnestly,' and '誠明' refers to sincerity and clarity of mind. Together, it suggests a rigorous pursuit of moral and spiritual purity.
  • 堂堂之陣，正正之旗 - A metaphor for a well-ordered and dignified approach, often used in military or philosophical contexts to describe a proper and honorable stance.
  • 知而不行，行而不專 - '知而不行' means knowing but not acting, and '行而不專' means acting without dedication. These phrases highlight the importance of both knowledge and commitment in practice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道人所云 - '道人' refers to a Daoist or a person who follows the Dao, often associated with spiritual or philosophical teachings. This phrase introduces a Daoist perspective on human nature and conduct.
  • 七情六欲 - '七情' refers to the seven emotions (joy, anger, sorrow, fear, love, hatred, and desire), and '六欲' refers to the six desires (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch, and thought). Together, they represent the full range of human emotions and desires, often seen as sources of distraction or temptation.
  • 鬼道 - Refers to the path of the dead or the underworld, symbolizing moral decline or spiritual corruption.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '人在陰陽之中，故可鬼可仙' introduces the central idea that humans occupy a middle ground between the divine and the demonic, capable of either spiritual elevation or moral decay.
  • The conditional clause '惟刻求誠明，自有四通八達之日' suggests that only through earnest pursuit of sincerity and clarity can one achieve spiritual enlightenment or moral clarity.
  • The sentence '任意荒怠，即中七情六欲之迷' contrasts the consequences of laziness and indulgence with the disciplined path of spiritual cultivation.
  • The metaphorical statement '道人所云，堂堂之陣，正正之旗也，明者自知' emphasizes the importance of a dignified and honorable approach to life, with the implication that true understanding comes from inner wisdom.
  • The final clause '知而不行，行而不專，是自趋于鬼道也' links the failure to act on knowledge or to act without dedication to the path of moral decline.
