Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次早 - Means 'the next morning' or 'early the next day.'
  • 倩邻妇呼其伙来 - '倩' means to ask or request, '邻妇' means neighbor woman, and '呼其伙来' means to call her companions. This phrase can be translated as 'the next morning, she asked her neighbor woman to call her companions.'
  • 昨夜仆与妇同逃 - '昨夜' means 'last night,' '仆与妇' means 'the husband and wife,' and '同逃' means 'fled together.' This phrase can be translated as 'last night, the husband and wife fled together.'
  • 幸未窃物去 - '幸' means 'fortunately,' '未' means 'not,' '窃物' means 'stole things,' and '去' means 'left.' This phrase can be translated as 'fortunately, they did not steal anything before leaving.'
  • 汝速遣人唤主人归 - '汝' means 'you,' '速' means 'quickly,' '遣人' means 'send someone,' '唤' means 'call,' and '主人归' means 'the owner to return.' This phrase can be translated as 'you quickly send someone to call the owner back.'
  • 商其事，合报官否 - '商其事' means 'discuss the matter,' '合' means 'should,' '报官' means 'report to the authorities,' and '否' means 'or not.' This phrase can be translated as 'discuss whether to report the matter to the authorities.'
  • 严含糊答对 - '严' refers to the owner, '含糊' means 'vague' or 'unclear,' and '答对' means 'answered.' This phrase can be translated as 'the owner vaguely answered.'
  • 夜深人静，方以杀奸事白之 - '夜深人静' means 'deep into the night when everyone was quiet,' '方' means 'only then,' '以' means 'with,' '杀奸事' means 'the act of killing the villain,' and '白之' means 'told him.' This phrase can be translated as 'deep into the night when everyone was quiet, he only then told him about the act of killing the villain.'
  • 张子愕然 - '张子' refers to the owner, '愕然' means 'startled' or 'shocked.' This phrase can be translated as 'the owner was startled.'
  • 君必首之，有罪妾任 - '君' means 'you,' '必' means 'must,' '首之' means 'report it,' '有罪' means 'if there is guilt,' and '妾任' means 'I will take responsibility.' This phrase can be translated as 'you must report it; if there is any guilt, I will take responsibility.'
  • 若潜隐其事，日久必破，君亦不得辞其咎矣 - '若' means 'if,' '潜隐其事' means 'conceal the matter,' '日久' means 'over time,' '必破' means 'it will be exposed,' '君' means 'you,' '亦' means 'also,' '不得辞其咎' means 'cannot escape blame,' and '矣' is a particle indicating completion. This phrase can be translated as 'if you conceal the matter, over time it will be exposed, and you also cannot escape blame.'
  • 遂首诸司坊 - '遂' means 'then,' '首' means 'report,' '诸司坊' means 'the relevant authorities.' This phrase can be translated as 'then reported to the relevant authorities.'
  • 官来验讯 - '官' means 'officials,' '来' means 'came,' '验讯' means 'investigated and questioned.' This phrase can be translated as 'officials came to investigate and question.'
  • 严氏侃侃直陈 - '严氏' refers to the wife, '侃侃' means 'calmly,' '直陈' means 'stated straightforwardly.' This phrase can be translated as 'the wife calmly stated straightforwardly.'
  • 奏交秋部大司寇鞠之 - '奏' means 'submitted,' '交' means 'to,' '秋部' refers to the Ministry of Punishment, '大司寇' is the highest judicial official, and '鞠之' means 'to try the case.' This phrase can be translated as 'submitted to the Ministry of Punishment for the highest judicial official to try the case.'
  • 严氏理直气壮，坚执不挠 - '严氏' refers to the wife, '理直气壮' means 'reasonably and confidently,' '坚执' means 'firmly held,' and '不挠' means 'unyielding.' This phrase can be translated as 'the wife was reasonably and confidently firm and unyielding.'
  • 乃释严氏夫妇而瘗二尸 - '乃' means 'then,' '释' means 'released,' '严氏夫妇' means 'the wife and her husband,' '而' means 'and,' '瘗' means 'buried,' and '二尸' means 'the two bodies.' This phrase can be translated as 'then released the wife and her husband and buried the two bodies.'
  • 案定后 - '案' means 'case,' '定' means 'settled,' and '后' means 'afterward.' This phrase can be translated as 'after the case was settled.'
  • 乡人之宦游京师者 - '乡人' means 'people from the village,' '之' is a possessive marker, '宦游' means 'serving in official posts,' and '京师' means 'the capital.' This phrase can be translated as 'people from the village who served in official posts in the capital.'
  • 咸羡严之胆智 - '咸' means 'all,' '羡' means 'admired,' '严之胆智' means 'the wife's courage and wisdom.' This phrase can be translated as 'all admired the wife's courage and wisdom.'
  • 公悬额以旌之 - '公' means 'officially,' '悬额' means 'hung a plaque,' and '以旌之' means 'to honor her.' This phrase can be translated as 'officially hung a plaque to honor her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仆与妇 - Refers to the husband and wife, a common way to refer to a married couple in classical Chinese.
  • 司坊 - Refers to local officials or magistrates who handle legal matters at the local level.
  • 秋部 - Refers to the Ministry of Punishment, one of the Six Ministries in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 大司寇 - The highest judicial official in the imperial court, responsible for trying major cases.
  • 案定 - Refers to the resolution of a legal case, often involving a formal judgment or verdict.
  • 乡人之宦游京师者 - Refers to people from the village who had served in official posts in the capital, indicating a connection between local and central government.
  • 悬额 - Refers to the practice of hanging plaques or inscriptions to honor individuals or events, a common form of public recognition in traditional Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 严氏 - Refers to the wife of the couple involved in the story.
  • 张子 - Refers to the owner of the property, likely the husband of the wife.
  • 司坊 - Refers to local officials or magistrates.
  • 秋部 - Refers to the Ministry of Punishment.
  • 大司寇 - Refers to the highest judicial official.
  • 乡人 - Refers to people from the village.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city.

[112]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 智明者，膽必壯 - A general statement that wise and discerning people tend to have boldness.
  • 初心 - Means 'original intention' or 'initial heart,' referring to the beginning state of mind.
  • 未嘗不呼嫠婦共拒 - Means 'has never failed to call out the widow to resist together,' indicating a repeated action.
  • 乃速轉關作蕩婦態 - Means 'then quickly change tactics to act like a wanton woman,' describing a sudden change in behavior.
  • 誘其入我彀中 - Means 'to lure her into my trap,' using a metaphor of ensnaring prey.
  • 刀可得，仇可報也 - Means 'the knife can be obtained, and the enemy can be avenged,' indicating a purposeful action.
  • 然亦有天幸 - Means 'but there is also a stroke of luck,' introducing an element of chance.
  • 若仆必欲先污之而後飲 - Means 'if you want to defile her first and then kill her,' indicating a specific intent.
  • 我知仆之命，必不能脫其手 - Means 'I know your life will not escape from my grasp,' expressing confidence in one's power.
  • 而嚴氏不得為完人 - Means 'and Yan will not remain a virtuous person,' indicating a moral consequence.
  • 飲恨無窮矣 - Means 'will harbor endless resentment,' expressing a lasting emotional state.
  • 必不被污也 - Means 'will certainly not be defiled,' indicating a determination.
  • 仆視嚴氏猶幾上肉 - Means 'I regard Yan as meat on the table,' using a metaphor to describe a vulnerable target.
  • 斷不能脫我口 - Means 'cannot escape my mouth,' indicating inevitability.
  • 何妨姑且作樂 - Means 'why not take pleasure in the meantime,' suggesting a temporary indulgence.
  • 況以兩情交暢之言動之 - Means 'especially when using words of mutual affection to move her,' indicating a persuasive approach.
  • 有不垂涎願從者乎 - Means 'who would not be tempted and willing to follow,' expressing a general tendency.
  • 從之，則必酩酊 - Means 'if she follows, she will surely become drunk,' indicating a predictable outcome.
  • 二人皆自取殺身也 - Means 'both will bring about their own deaths,' expressing a fatal consequence.
  • 我願世之君子室無嚴氏 - Means 'I wish that gentlemen's households do not have women like Yan,' expressing a desire for avoidance.
  • 斷不可使少婦壯仆在一处 - Means 'definitely do not allow young women and strong servants to be together,' indicating a precautionary measure.
  • 即有嚴氏，多一事，不如少一事也 - Means 'even if there is a Yan, having fewer things is better than having more,' expressing a preference for simplicity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 智明者 - Refers to wise and discerning individuals.
  • 初心 - Refers to the initial state of mind or intention.
  • 嫠婦 - Refers to a widow.
  • 蕩婦 - Refers to a wanton or promiscuous woman.
  • 嚴氏 - Refers to a woman named Yan.
  • 完人 - Refers to a virtuous or morally complete person.
  • 飲恨 - Refers to harboring deep resentment.
  • 少婦壯仆 - Refers to young women and strong servants, indicating a potential source of conflict.

Proper Nouns

  • 嚴氏 - The name of a woman, Yan.
  • 僕 - A term for a servant or subordinate.

[113]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 潘翁 - Respectful title for an elderly man named Pan; '翁' can be translated as 'old man' or 'elder.'
  • 粤东香山人 - '粤东' refers to the eastern part of Guangdong province, and '香山' is the name of a county, now known as Zhongshan City; '人' means 'person' or 'native.'
  • 谈者忘其名字 - '谈者' refers to people who talk about him, and '忘其名字' means they forget his name; suggests Pan翁 is well-known for his deeds rather than his name.
  • 生在前明中叶 - '前明' means 'before the Ming Dynasty,' and '中叶' means 'middle period'; indicates Pan翁 lived during the middle period of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 家赀千万 - '家赀' means 'family wealth,' and '千万' means 'ten thousand'; indicates Pan翁 was extremely wealthy.
  • 席丰履厚 - Classical idiom meaning 'to enjoy great wealth and comfort'; describes someone who is prosperous and comfortable.
  • 有善必为 - '有善' means 'good deeds,' and '必为' means 'must do'; means Pan翁 always performs good deeds.
  • 刷印经文、创修庙宇、造桥铺路、掩骼埋胔 - Specific good deeds: '刷印经文' means 'printing scriptures,' '创修庙宇' means 'building temples,' '造桥铺路' means 'building bridges and roads,' and '掩骼埋胔' means 'burying the bones of the dead.'
  • 赠亲朋、济邻里 - '赠亲朋' means 'giving gifts to relatives and friends,' and '济邻里' means 'helping neighbors.'
  • 日食千百人 - '日食' means 'daily food,' and '千百人' means 'thousands of people'; indicates Pan翁 feeds thousands of people daily.
  • 不使邑有乞丐 - '不使' means 'not to let,' '邑' means 'town,' and '乞丐' means 'beggars'; means Pan翁 ensures there are no beggars in the town.
  • 旱涝之年 - '旱涝' means 'drought and flood,' and '之年' means 'year'; refers to years of natural disasters.
  • 远近就哺 - '远近' means 'near and far,' and '就哺' means 'seeking food'; means people from far and near come to seek food.
  • 日以万计 - '日' means 'daily,' and '以万计' means 'in the tens of thousands'; means thousands of people come daily.
  • 翁虽富，力渐不支 - '翁' means 'old man,' '虽' means 'although,' '富' means 'rich,' '力' means 'strength,' and '渐不支' means 'gradually unable to support'; means despite his wealth, Pan翁 gradually becomes unable to support the needs.
  • 去质库，卖田宅为之 - '去质库' means 'to pawn things,' '卖田宅' means 'to sell land and houses,' and '为之' means 'to do it'; means Pan翁 pawns things and sells his land and houses to continue helping others.
  • 时和年丰 - '时和' means 'good times,' and '年丰' means 'abundant harvest'; means there is a good harvest and favorable conditions.
  • 灾黎散而善人贫矣 - '灾黎' means 'disaster-stricken people,' '散' means 'dispersed,' '善人' means 'good people,' and '贫矣' means 'poor'; means the disaster-stricken people have dispersed, and Pan翁 has become poor.
  • 媪旦暮必至 - '媪' means 'old woman,' '旦暮' means 'morning and evening,' and '必至' means 'must come'; means the old woman comes every morning and evening.
  • 索食饭之，索饮茗之 - '索食' means 'ask for food,' '饭之' means 'give her food,' '索饮' means 'ask for drink,' and '茗之' means 'give her tea'; means the old woman asks for food and drink, and Pan翁 gives them to her.
  • 无倦色 - '无倦色' means 'without showing any sign of fatigue'; means the old woman does not show any sign of tiredness.
  • 如是者久 - '如是' means 'like this,' '者' means 'one,' and '久' means 'long'; means this situation has continued for a long time.
  • 无如旱涝之年 - '无如' means 'not like,' and '旱涝之年' means 'years of drought and flood'; means this situation is not like the years of natural disasters.
  • 闻善人之名者，远近就哺 - '闻善人之名者' means 'those who hear of the good man's name,' '远近' means 'near and far,' and '就哺' means 'seeking food'; means people from far and near come to seek food because they know of Pan翁's good deeds.
  • 翁虽富，力渐不支 - '翁' means 'old man,' '虽' means 'although,' '富' means 'rich,' '力' means 'strength,' and '渐不支' means 'gradually unable to support'; means despite his wealth, Pan翁 gradually becomes unable to support the needs.
  • 去质库，卖田宅为之 - '去质库' means 'to pawn things,' '卖田宅' means 'to sell land and houses,' and '为之' means 'to do it'; means Pan翁 pawns things and sells his land and houses to continue helping others.
  • 迨时和年丰 - '迨' means 'until,' '时和' means 'good times,' and '年丰' means 'abundant harvest'; means until there is a good harvest and favorable conditions.
  • 灾黎散而善人贫矣 - '灾黎' means 'disaster-stricken people,' '散' means 'dispersed,' '善人' means 'good people,' and '贫矣' means 'poor'; means the disaster-stricken people have dispersed, and Pan翁 has become poor.
  • 前媪尚日诣之 - '前媪' means 'the old woman,' '尚' means 'still,' '日' means 'daily,' and '诣之' means 'go to him'; means the old woman still goes to see Pan翁 daily.
  • 翁犹自减口粮以为之食 - '翁' means 'old man,' '犹' means 'still,' '自减口粮' means 'reduce his own food,' and '以为之食' means 'to give her food'; means Pan翁 still reduces his own food to give her food.
  • 始终不怠 - '始终' means 'always,' and '不怠' means 'not slack off'; means Pan翁 never slackens off.
  • 媪不自安 - '媪' means 'old woman,' and '不自安' means 'feels uneasy'; means the old woman feels uneasy.
  • 谓翁曰 - '谓' means 'say,' and '翁曰' means 'the old man said'; means the old woman says to Pan翁.
  • 我孤独无归之嫠也 - '我' means 'I,' '孤独' means 'lonely,' '无归' means 'no place to return,' and '嫠' means 'widow'; means she is a lonely widow with no place to return.
  • 今汝业已败 - '今' means 'now,' '汝' means 'you,' '业' means 'business,' and '已败' means 'already ruined'; means now your business is already ruined.
  • 我何忍累汝 - '我' means 'I,' '何忍' means 'how can I bear,' '累' means 'burden,' and '汝' means 'you'; means how can I bear to burden you.
  • 曷以住宅施我作大士阁 - '曷' means 'how,' '以' means 'to,' '住宅' means 'house,' '施' means 'give,' '我' means 'me,' and '作大士阁' means 'build a Bodhisattva pavilion'; means how about giving me your house to build a Bodhisattva pavilion.
  • 我愿为优婆夷 - '我' means 'I,' '愿' means 'wish,' and '优婆夷' means 'laywoman'; means I wish to be a laywoman.
  • 自主香火 - '主' means 'manage,' and '香火' means 'incense and candles'; means manage the incense and candles.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 前明 - Refers to the period before the Ming Dynasty.
  • 香山 - Name of a county, now known as Zhongshan City.
  • 优婆夷 - Laywoman in Buddhism.

Proper Nouns

  • 潘翁 - Respectful title for an elderly man named Pan.
  • 粤东 - Eastern part of Guangdong province.
  • 香山 - County, now known as Zhongshan City.
  • 媪 - Old woman.
  • 大士阁 - Bodhisattva pavilion.

[114]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 北郭 - Northern outskirts of a city.
  • 数顷 - A few acres (a unit of land measurement).
  • 因历年旱涝，寸草不生 - Due to years of drought and flooding, not a blade of grass grows.
  • 无人肯售 - No one is willing to sell it.
  • 去结茅庐亦足栖止 - If you build a thatched hut there, it is sufficient for habitation.
  • 何怨之有 - What is there to complain about?
  • 此地吾前见之 - This place I have seen before.
  • 有大风水在 - There is a great feng shui here.
  • 汝因历年为善 - You have been virtuous over the years.
  • 未及葬汝父母 - You have not yet buried your parents.
  • 今既已迁此 - Now that you have moved here.
  • 我当为卜宅 - I will predict the auspiciousness of this place.
  • 其山之阳，枯木之下，天生石穴一区 - On the sunny side of the mountain, under a dead tree, there is a naturally formed stone cave.
  • 直佳城也 - It is a good place for a tomb.
  • 窀穸于此 - Bury your parents here.
  • 不但旧业可复 - Not only can your old business be restored.
  • 将亿万斯年，吃着不尽 - For countless years, you will have more than enough to eat and live on.
  • 汝信之乎 - Do you believe this?
  • 谨受救 - I will follow your advice.
  • 媪执引送之 - The old woman leads you there.
  • 指牛眠穴 - Point out the cow's resting place.
  • 坎山离向 - Facing south with the back to the north.
  • 以罗浮为案 - With the Lufu Mountains as a screen.
  • 以大海为荫 - With the sea as a shelter.
  • 此凡夫之所见不及者也 - This is something that ordinary people cannot see.
  • 且使庐墓而居 - And you can live in a thatched hut near the tomb.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北郭 - Northern outskirts of a city, often associated with rural or less developed areas.
  • 山地 - Mountainous land, which can be challenging to cultivate due to its terrain.
  • 旱涝 - Drought and flooding, natural disasters that can affect agriculture and land use.
  • 茅庐 - Thatched hut, a simple and humble dwelling.
  • 风水 - Feng shui, the traditional Chinese practice of harmonizing individuals with their surrounding environment.
  • 卜宅 - Predicting the auspiciousness of a place, often using feng shui principles.
  • 牛眠穴 - A term in feng shui referring to an ideal burial site for a tomb.
  • 坎山离向 - A feng shui orientation where the tomb faces south with the back to the north.
  • 罗浮 - Lufu Mountains, a famous mountain range in Guangdong Province known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 大海 - The sea, often symbolizing vastness and protection in Chinese culture.

[115]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃出异果数斗 - Literal phrase meaning 'then produced several baskets of strange fruit,' indicating a generous and significant gift.
  • 给之曰 - Means 'gave them and said,' introducing the speaker's instructions.
  • 以此遍撒荒田 - Means 'use this to scatter over barren fields,' suggesting a method of cultivation.
  • 俟其生长勿伐 - Means 'wait for it to grow without cutting it down,' emphasizing the need for patience and care.
  • 三年乃成 - Means 'it will take three years to mature,' indicating a long-term process.
  • 三年后，必有蛇虺守此 - Means 'after three years, there will definitely be snakes and venomous snakes guarding this place,' suggesting a protective or mystical element.
  • 汝当别迁 - Means 'you should move away,' advising the recipient to relocate.
  • 欲得此异物者 - Means 'if you want to obtain this strange thing,' introducing a condition.
  • 惟五月五日 - Means 'only on the fifth day of the fifth month,' specifying a particular time.
  • 可使健男子 - Means 'you can have a strong man,' indicating the type of person needed.
  • 身涂雄黄 - Means 'cover his body with realgar,' describing a specific action.
  • 醉饱来伐取 - Means 'come drunk and full to cut it down,' suggesting a ritualistic or symbolic act.
  • 行将独擅天下之妙业矣 - Means 'you will then uniquely possess the wonderful art of the world,' implying a significant achievement.
  • 将为何物？必先教之 - Means 'what will it be? You must first learn it,' indicating a request for information and instruction.
  • 汝将来观其形状，似何物即创为何物 - Means 'you will see its shape later, and whatever it resembles, you should create accordingly,' suggesting a creative process.
  • 何必先知？ - Means 'why must you know it beforehand?' questioning the necessity of prior knowledge.
  • 但我所与者，菩提果也 - Means 'but what I give you is the Bodhi fruit,' identifying the specific gift.
  • 勿以予人 - Means 'do not give it to others,' emphasizing the exclusivity of the gift.
  • 纵予人，无益 - Means 'even if you give it to others, it will be of no benefit,' reinforcing the exclusivity.
  • 媪乃去 - Means 'the old woman then left,' indicating the conclusion of the interaction.
  • 翁于是安庐种果 - Means 'the old man then settled down to live in a hut and cultivate the fruit,' describing the recipient's actions.
  • 半载方休 - Means 'after half a year, he stopped,' indicating a period of activity.
  • 入城视己旧居 - Means 'he went into the city to see his old residence,' describing a return to a familiar place.
  • 已成观音堂 - Means 'it had already become a Guanyin hall,' indicating a transformation of the place.
  • 香火甚盛 - Means 'the incense and fire were very prosperous,' suggesting a thriving religious site.
  • 有尼住持 - Means 'there was a nun in charge,' identifying the caretaker.
  • 媪已不知去向矣 - Means 'the old woman was no longer known where she went,' indicating her disappearance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 菩提果 - Refers to the Bodhi fruit, a fruit associated with enlightenment in Buddhist tradition.
  • 蛇虺 - Refers to snakes and venomous snakes, often associated with protection or danger in Chinese folklore.
  • 五月五日 - Refers to the fifth day of the fifth month of the lunar calendar, known as the Dragon Boat Festival, a significant traditional Chinese holiday.
  • 雄黄 - Refers to realgar, a mineral used in traditional Chinese medicine and rituals, often associated with warding off evil spirits.
  • 观音堂 - Refers to a temple dedicated to Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of Compassion, a central figure in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 尼 - Refers to a nun, indicating the religious nature of the caretaker.

Proper Nouns

  • 菩提果 - The Bodhi fruit.
  • 蛇虺 - Snakes and venomous snakes.
  • 五月五日 - The fifth day of the fifth month of the lunar calendar.
  • 雄黄 - Realgar.
  • 观音堂 - A temple dedicated to Guanyin.
  • 尼 - A nun.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翁归不数月 - Refers to a short period of time after the old man returns.
  • 其物萌茁 - Describes the vigorous growth of something, likely a plant or tree.
  • 形似芭蕉 - Means 'in shape like a banana plant.'
  • 遍山皆是 - Means 'all over the mountain,' indicating widespread presence.
  • 爱护之，勿敢伐 - Means 'to love and protect it, not daring to cut it down.'
  • 历三年 - Means 'over three years,' indicating a prolonged period.
  • 叶形成扇 - Means 'the leaves form like fans,' describing the shape of the leaves.
  • 翁乃剥其粗者，贱售之 - Means 'the old man then peeled the coarse ones and sold them cheaply.'
  • 人皆争市 - Means 'people all competed to buy,' indicating high demand.
  • 一夏得数万钱 - Means 'in one summer, he earned tens of thousands of coins,' indicating significant financial gain.
  • 时已有巨蛇出没 - Means 'at that time, there were already large snakes appearing,' indicating the presence of dangerous creatures.
  • 毒气射人 - Means 'toxic gas shooting at people,' describing the danger posed by the snakes.
  • 翁不能存身，仍迁城市 - Means 'the old man could not stay there, so he moved to the city,' indicating a forced relocation.
  • 次年，如媪嘱 - Means 'the next year, as instructed by the old woman,' indicating following someone's advice.

  • 他人欲窃效之，俱不能 - Means 'others tried to secretly imitate him, but none succeeded,' indicating the uniqueness of the method.

  • 故无敢窃 - Means 'so no one dared to steal,' indicating the deterrent effect.
  • 亲友乞其种，亦未尝吝惜 - Means 'relatives and friends asked for its seeds, and he never hesitated to give them,' indicating generosity.
  • 但树他处，变为美人蕉 - Means 'but when planted elsewhere, it turned into a beauty banana,' indicating a change in the plant.
  • 结花娇红可爱 - Means 'with beautiful red flowers that are lovely,' describing the appearance of the flowers.

  • 传翁绝业，为天下后世为善者示劝 - Means 'transmitting the old man's unique business, showing encouragement to those who do good in the world,' indicating a moral lesson.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 十二司戴元首，合而为身毒国 - Metaphorical expression where '十二司' (twelve departments) and '元首' (head) combine to form '身毒国' (Shendu), meaning a country governed by twelve departments.
  • 毒译督，言一司总督诸司也 - '毒' is a pun for '督' (supervise), and '言一司总督诸司也' means 'it is said that one department supervises all other departments.'
  • 耳目口鼻喉舌最贵 - Metaphorically, 'ear' and 'eye' for information gathering, 'mouth' and 'nose' for communication, 'throat' and 'tongue' for speech and command.
  • 比中华之宰相尚书卿贰 - '宰相' (prime minister), '尚书' (minister), '卿贰' (vice-ministers) are high-ranking officials in the Chinese government.
  • 左右肱比都督将军 - '左右肱' (left and right arms) metaphorically represent military leaders or generals.
  • 心腹比抚按 - '心腹' (heart and belly) represent trusted advisors or ministers.
  • 臂指比提镇 - '臂指' (arm and finger) represent local governors or commanders.
  • 前阳主刑名，后阴主钱谷 - '前阳' (front yang) and '后阴' (rear yin) are metaphors for the division of responsibilities, with '刑名' (punishment and names) and '钱谷' (money and grain) representing legal and financial matters.
  • 左右股比汛弁 - '左右股' (left and right thighs) represent lower-ranking military officers.
  • 皮毛司比佐贰杂职 - '皮毛司' (skin and hair departments) represent minor or auxiliary positions.
  • 自王以下，皆布衣 - '布衣' (common clothes) indicates that everyone, including the king, wears simple clothing.
  • 以色分品级 - '色' (color) is used to distinguish ranks, with '黄炎' (yellow and red), '朱' (red), '蓝' (blue), '青' (green), and '白' (white) representing different levels.
  • 田地无主，亦无旷土 - '无主' (no owner) and '无旷土' (no idle land) indicate that land is well-managed and cultivated.
  • 产香谷，大于中华之稻米 - '香谷' (fragrant grain) and '大于中华之稻米' (larger than Chinese rice) describe a type of grain that is more abundant and fragrant than Chinese rice.
  • 一岁三收 - '一岁三收' means three harvests per year.
  • 至春夏秋三时，人之有力者，不拘何地，尽量布种 - '至春夏秋三时' (during the spring, summer, and autumn seasons) and '人之有力者，不拘何地，尽量布种' (people with strength, regardless of location, plant as much as possible) describe the agricultural practices.
  • 诸司督耕，能多得谷者，司举奏王，以下司爵之，有递迁至朱衣者 - '诸司督耕' (all departments supervise farming), '能多得谷者' (those who can produce more grain), '司举奏王' (departments report to the king), '下司爵之' (promote to a lower rank), '有递迁至朱衣者' (some may be promoted to red rank).
  • 第非考较出身，仅虚职荣之耳 - '第非考较出身' (not based on examination results) and '仅虚职荣之耳' (only honorary positions) indicate that promotions are honorary rather than based on merit.
  • 怠情者，发穷黎岛，日给一粥终其身 - '怠情者' (lazy people), '发穷黎岛' (sent to the poor island), '日给一粥终其身' (given one bowl of porridge per day for life) describe the punishment for laziness.

  • 谋食 - Means 'seeking sustenance' or 'looking for a job,' implying that the scholar-official is trying to find employment.
  • 新得宠姬某氏 - Means 'recently acquired a favored concubine,' indicating that the official has a new favorite among his secondary wives.
  • 诗笔清新 - Means 'her poetry is fresh and clear,' describing the quality of the concubine's writing.
  • 与孝廉相类 - Means 'similar to the scholar-official,' indicating that the concubine's poetry shares qualities with the scholar-official's.
  • 遂使姬作女学生 - Means 'he then had the concubine pretend to be a female student,' suggesting a ruse to facilitate their relationship.

  • 值制军寿诞 - Means 'on the occasion of the military governor's birthday,' indicating a specific event.

  • 次日，仆妇书童，报二人夜不归室之事，制军大怒 - Means 'the next day, the servants and the scribe reported that the two did not return to their rooms at night, and the military governor became very angry,' indicating the discovery of their escape.
  • 传心腹武弁入内室，授之剑而谕之故曰 - Means 'he summoned a trusted military officer into his inner chamber, gave him a sword, and explained the reason,' indicating the official's command.

  • 认姬作汝妻，被孝廉奸拐，故追而斩之 - Means 'pretend that the concubine is your wife and that she was abducted by the scholar-official, so you can chase and kill him,' indicating the cover story.

  • 弁领命将行，夫人遽出喝止 - Means 'the military officer was about to leave, but the lady of the house suddenly came out and shouted to stop him,' indicating her intervention.

  • 机事不密，则害成 - Means 'if secret matters are not kept confidential, they will cause harm,' indicating the importance of discretion.
  • 此等事，想已扬播省垣矣 - Means 'such matters must have already spread throughout the provincial capital,' indicating the likely spread of news.
  • 引盗入室，斩之非义 - Means 'inviting a thief into your home and killing him is unjust,' indicating the moral wrongness of the action.
  • 一顶绿头巾，终脱不去，徒然掩耳盗铃，不贻笑士林耶 - Means 'wearing a green headband (a symbol of infidelity) will never be shaken off, it's just like plugging one's ears while stealing a bell, and it will make you a laughingstock among scholars,' indicating the consequences of the action.
  • 使士林抱不平，察实而发其覆，贻害无穷矣 - Means 'if scholars feel unjustly treated, they will investigate and expose the truth, causing endless harm,' indicating the potential backlash.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 即作书以四百金 - Formal expression indicating the immediate act of writing a letter and sending it with a gift of four hundred gold pieces.
  • 遣奴子追赠 - Means 'sent a servant to catch up and deliver the gift,' indicating urgency or importance.
  • 仆年逾花甲 - Formal statement meaning 'I am over sixty years old,' using the idiom '花甲' to denote age.
  • 非自好也 - Means 'not because I am fond of it,' indicating that the action is not for personal pleasure.
  • 诚以聪明俊秀之产，造物亦几费经营 - Formal expression meaning 'indeed, the creation of intelligent and handsome offspring also requires considerable effort,' suggesting that the creation of such individuals is a significant endeavor.
  • 使之误配莽夫，殊深惋惜 - Means 'if they were to be paired with a coarse man, it would be deeply regrettable,' expressing concern for the potential misfortune.
  • 是以罗而致之 - Means 'therefore, I gathered them together,' indicating a deliberate action to bring them together.
  • 且随其姿质，教成学术 - Means 'and according to their talents, I taught them scholarly pursuits,' emphasizing the tailored education.
  • 以配君子 - Means 'to match them with a gentleman,' indicating a desire for a suitable match.
  • 岂非天下大快事耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'isn't this a great joy in the world?' expressing the author's satisfaction.
  • 所不预言者 - Means 'what I did not predict,' indicating an unexpected event.
  • 缘此女学未大成 - Means 'because this girl's learning was not yet complete,' explaining the delay in the match.
  • 欲其精进而后成之 - Means 'I wanted her to refine her studies and then complete them,' indicating a desire for further development.
  • 不意巫山神女，竞自投楚王矣 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'unintentionally, the goddess of Wuchang fell into the arms of King Chu,' indicating an unexpected turn of events.
  • 其识见亦属非凡 - Means 'her insight is also extraordinary,' praising the girl's intelligence.
  • 仆当顺其情，以遂好逑佳话 - Means 'I will follow her feelings to fulfill this good match,' indicating a decision based on the girl's wishes.
  • 但不别而行 - Means 'but without saying goodbye,' indicating an unexpected departure.
  • 妙手空空，腰缠何藉 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'with empty hands, how can one rely on the money in the belt,' indicating a lack of resources.
  • 未免累及君子 - Means 'it cannot help but burden the gentleman,' expressing concern for the gentleman's situation.
  • 故遣仆走馈赆 - Means 'therefore, I sent a servant to deliver a gift,' indicating a practical solution.
  • 伏冀哂存 - Formal expression meaning 'I hope you will accept it with a smile,' expressing a polite request.
  • 所有足下行囊，与某女衣饰，俱捡点附上 - Means 'all your belongings and the girl's clothes have been carefully checked and attached,' indicating a thorough preparation.

  • This clause is a complex sentence that can be broken down into 'I am over sixty years old, and the reason I have a young daughter is not because I am fond of it.'
  • This clause is a long sentence that can be broken down into 'indeed, the creation of intelligent and handsome offspring also requires considerable effort, and if they were to be paired with a coarse man, it would be deeply regrettable.'
  • This clause is a series of actions that can be broken down into 'therefore, I gathered them together, and according to their talents, I taught them scholarly pursuits, to match them with a gentleman.'
  • This clause is a rhetorical question that can be read as 'isn't this a great joy in the world?'
  • This clause can be read as 'what I did not predict is that this girl's learning was not yet complete.'
  • This clause can be read as 'I wanted her to refine her studies and then complete them.'

  • 已渝妾奏，得近云门 - A metaphorical expression indicating that they have been allowed to approach the teacher's teachings, likening it to a woman being allowed to enter the imperial court.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 年余 - General time expression meaning 'after some time' or 'several years'.
  • 得之矣 - Colloquial expression meaning 'I have it' or 'I have found it'.
  • 遍历荒山 - Literally 'traverse the wilderness mountains,' meaning 'to explore the wild mountains'.
  • 得无主之地数十顷 - Literally 'obtain ownerless land for tens of hectares,' meaning 'to acquire unclaimed land covering tens of hectares'.
  • 尽其所有，皆易钱 - Literally 'sell all his possessions for money,' meaning 'to sell all his belongings for cash'.
  • 乞贷父兄亲族以益之 - Literally 'borrow from his father, brothers, and relatives to supplement it,' meaning 'to borrow from his family and relatives to add to the amount'.
  • 使生置芋粟 - Literally 'have the man plant yams and millet,' meaning 'to have the man cultivate yams and millet'.
  • 一名苞谷 - Literally 'also known as corn,' meaning 'also known as maize'.
  • 此贱而易成之物 - Literally 'this cheap and easily grown thing,' meaning 'this inexpensive and easily cultivated crop'.
  • 遍撤山地 - Literally 'spread across the mountain land,' meaning 'to plant it throughout the mountainous area'.
  • 日游里闾 - Literally 'daily wander through the village,' meaning 'to frequently visit the village'.
  • 结好众瑶妇 - Literally 'befriend the many Yao women,' meaning 'to build good relations with the Yao women'.
  • 得其爱戴心 - Literally 'gain their affection and respect,' meaning 'to earn their love and admiration'.
  • 谓之曰 - Literally 'tell them,' meaning 'to say to them'.
  • 本年当大旱 - Literally 'this year will be a great drought,' meaning 'this year is expected to be very dry'.
  • 救荒之计，我已密布山间 - Literally 'plans to save from famine, I have secretly spread in the mountains,' meaning 'my plan to alleviate famine is already in place in the mountains'.
  • 将来成熟时，可以周济汝等 - Literally 'when it matures in the future, it can help you all,' meaning 'when it ripens, it will be able to help you all'.
  • 但须为我照料耳 - Literally 'just need to take care of it for me,' meaning 'just need to look after it for me'.
  • 众妇皆悦，为之挟刃巡逻 - Literally 'the women all agreed, and they carried weapons to patrol,' meaning 'the women all agreed and took turns patrolling with weapons'.
  • 秀实时 - Literally 'when it is ripe,' meaning 'when it is ready to harvest'.
  • 争为收割 - Literally 'compete to harvest,' meaning 'rival to gather the crops'.
  • 不失一茎 - Literally 'not miss a single stem,' meaning 'to not leave a single plant unharvested'.
  • 时果夏秋无雨 - Literally 'it turned out that there was no rain in summer and autumn,' meaning 'it turned out that there was no rain during the summer and autumn seasons'.
  • 禾苗枯槁 - Literally 'the crops wither,' meaning 'the crops withered'.
  • 惟此独茂 - Literally 'only this one thrives,' meaning 'only this one flourished'.
  • 计口授瑶妇粟 - Literally 'distribute millet according to the number of mouths,' meaning 'to distribute millet based on the number of people'.
  • 欢呼拜谢而去 - Literally 'they cheered and bowed in gratitude and left,' meaning 'they cheered, bowed in gratitude, and departed'.
  • 尚余千锺 - Literally 'there were still a thousand dou left,' meaning 'there were still a thousand dou remaining'.
  • 粜之，得千余金 - Literally 'sell it, and get more than a thousand gold pieces,' meaning 'sell it and earn more than a thousand gold pieces'.
  • 偿债之外，犹称小康 - Literally 'after paying off debts, still considered moderately prosperous,' meaning 'after paying off debts, he was still considered moderately well-off'.
  • 次年，瑶妇皆来请种，愿为耕耘 - Literally 'the next year, the Yao women all came to ask to plant, willing to cultivate,' meaning 'the next year, the Yao women all came to ask to plant and were willing to cultivate'.
  • 今年应涝，惟稷独成 - Literally 'this year should be a flood, only millet will succeed,' meaning 'this year is expected to be flooded, but only millet will succeed'.
  • 购种遍播之 - Literally 'buy seeds and spread them everywhere,' meaning 'to buy seeds and plant them widely'.
  • 夏秋果大雨 - Literally 'in summer and autumn, it indeed rained heavily,' meaning 'in summer and autumn, it rained heavily'.
  • 诸谷皆淹没 - Literally 'all other grains were submerged,' meaning 'all other grains were flooded'.
  • 稷高丈余，不畏水 - Literally 'millet grew over ten feet tall, not afraid of water,' meaning 'millet grew over ten feet tall and was not afraid of water'.
  • 又获丰收 - Literally 'and had another bountiful harvest,' meaning 'and had another abundant harvest'.
  • 仍分给酬劳外，剩数千锺 - Literally 'still distributed rewards, and had several thousand dou left,' meaning 'still distributed rewards and had several thousand dou remaining'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 察官民皆乏食 - 察 means to observe or investigate, and 乏食 means lacking food. Together, this phrase indicates that the official observed both officials and common people were suffering from food shortages.
  • 流为盗贼 - 流 means to flow or become, and 盗贼 refers to thieves or bandits. This phrase suggests that due to the food shortage, people turned to crime.
  • 赖以无恙 - 赖 means to rely on, and 无恙 means to be unharmed or unaffected. This phrase indicates that the area (presumably the region under the official's jurisdiction) remained safe and unaffected by the crisis.
  • 男女咸敬佩良玉如神明 - 咸 means all or both, and 敬佩 means to respect and admire. 如神明 means like gods or deities. This phrase suggests that both men and women deeply respected and admired the official named Liang Yu as if he were a deity.
  • 愿为服役者甚众 - 愿为服役 means to be willing to serve, and 甚众 means very many. This phrase indicates that many people were eager to serve him.
  • 择其勤能者留之 - 择 means to select, 勤能 means diligent and capable, and 留之 means to keep them. This phrase suggests that Liang Yu chose the diligent and capable individuals to retain.
  • 何以知天支，测之皆验 - 何以知 means how to know, 天支 refers to the heavenly mandate or signs, 测之 means to test or measure, and 皆验 means all are verified. This phrase asks how the official knew about the heavenly signs and confirms that all his predictions were accurate.
  • 巢居知风，穴居知雨 - 巢居 means to live in nests (birds), 知风 means to know the wind, 穴居 means to live in holes (animals), and 知雨 means to know the rain. This phrase is a metaphorical expression suggesting that those who live in nests know the wind, and those who live in holes know the rain, implying that Liang Yu learned to predict weather patterns by observing ants.
  • 我师蚁耳 - 师 means to learn from, 蚁 means ants, and 耳 is a particle indicating emphasis. This phrase means that Liang Yu learned from ants.
  • 凡旱年，其穴必深，涝年，必迁高处 - 凡 means all, 旱年 means drought years, 其穴必深 means their nests must be deep, 涝年 means flood years, and 必迁高处 means they must move to higher places. This phrase explains that during drought years, ants build deeper nests, and during flood years, they move to higher ground.
  • 百不失一 - 百 means hundred, 不失 means not to lose, and 一 means one. This phrase means that Liang Yu's predictions were always correct.
  • 何须高谈天文，炫奇惊异哉 - 何须 means why need, 高谈 means to speak highly, 天文 means astronomy, 炫奇 means to show off strange things, and 惊异 means to amaze. This phrase suggests that there was no need to talk about astronomy or show off strange phenomena when he could predict accurately.
  • 卿言富，则果富矣。贵乌在 - 卿 is a respectful address, 言富 means to say rich, 则果富矣 means then indeed he is rich, and 贵乌在 means where is the nobility. This phrase indicates that the official is indeed rich, but asks where his nobility lies.
  • 勿急，我后必貤封君，为天下妇人吐气 - 勿急 means do not hurry, 我后 means in the future, 必貤封君 means will definitely be ennobled, and 为天下妇人吐气 means to speak on behalf of all women. This phrase reassures the questioner that in the future, he will be ennobled and speak on behalf of all women.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 良玉 - The name of the official, Liang Yu, who is being discussed.
  • 貤封君 - 貤封 means to confer titles posthumously, and 君 means lord or nobleman. This phrase suggests that Liang Yu will be posthumously ennobled.
  • 为天下妇人吐气 - This phrase reflects the social status of women in ancient China, where women often had limited rights and were often spoken for by men. Liang Yu's promise to speak on behalf of all women indicates his commitment to improving their status.

Proper Nouns

  • 良玉 - The name of the official, Liang Yu.
  • 石柱 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific region or city.
  • 貤封君 - A title indicating posthumous ennoblement.
  • 天下妇人 - Refers to all women, reflecting the social context of the time.

  • 土司驱市人而战，大败，遂杀土司 - 土司驱赶市人作战，大败，于是杀了土司 or the chieftain drove the townspeople into battle, suffered a great defeat, and was subsequently killed. Describes the outcome of the battle.
  • 寨民大恐，公议良玉之夫，系土司宗戚，应袭职 - 寨民大为恐慌，公议良玉的丈夫，他是土司的宗亲，应继承职位 or the villagers were greatly alarmed and publicly discussed that Liang Yu's husband, who was related to the chieftain, should inherit the position. Describes the villagers' reaction and the proposed solution.

  • 玉曰：「御侮之道，须众齐心，如臂指之相使，乃克有功。若人各一心，前车可鉴，是死我矣。君辈能听我号令否？」 - 玉说：‘抵御外侮的方法，必须大家齐心协力，像手臂和手指一样互相配合，才能成功。如果每个人各自为政，前车之鉴就在眼前，那就会害死我。你们能听从我的命令吗？’ or Liang Yu said, 'The way to defend against the enemy is for everyone to work together, like the arm and fingers working in unison, to achieve success. If everyone acts independently, the lessons of the past are clear, and that would be my death. Can you all follow my orders?' Describes Liang Yu's reasoning and her request for obedience.
  • 众皆曰：「凡我寨民，所以得温饱者，皆出夫人之赐。谁不愿为效命？如有异志，众共戳之。」 - 众人说：‘凡是我们的寨民，之所以能温饱，都是夫人的恩赐。谁不愿意为她效力？如果有异心，大家共同惩罚他。’ or the people said, 'All the villagers who have been able to live comfortably are due to the lady's grace. Who would not be willing to serve her? If anyone has different intentions, we will all punish them together.' Describes the villagers' pledge of loyalty.
  • 良玉察其情切，乃出视事 - 良玉看到他们情真意切，于是出来处理事务 or Liang Yu saw their sincerity and came out to handle the affairs. Describes Liang Yu's response to the villagers' plea.
  • 先点千人，各予连弩一张，一发十矢，命其兄统领，射退乌合之师 - 先挑选一千人，每人发一张连弩，一发十箭，命令她的哥哥统领，射退了乌合之师 or she first selected a thousand people, gave each a repeating crossbow that could fire ten arrows at a time, and ordered her brother to lead them, driving back the disorganized enemy forces. Describes Liang Yu's strategy and action.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生大悦曰 - Classical way of introducing a character's speech; 'Greatly pleased, he said.'
  • 方贼之劫寨，卿何预知之 - Question asking about foreknowledge; 'When the bandits were attacking the camp, how did you know in advance?'
  • 得天观地听力也 - Phrase indicating the use of natural signs and sounds to predict events; 'Through observing the sky, the earth, and listening to sounds.'
  • 牲畜在前，土车随后 - Describing the formation of the bandits' approach; 'Livestock in front, earth carts following.'
  • 早以见闻得报 - Indicating that the situation was anticipated; 'Early reports were received through observation and hearing.'
  • 我知此法，前寨必破，故退伏于外，而以地雷火炮伏寨中 - Explaining the strategic decision to retreat and set up defenses; 'I knew this method, so I expected the previous camp to be breached, and thus withdrew to the outside while setting up landmines and firecrackers inside the camp.'
  • 彼见寨多粮食，谅必停留 - Indicating the bandits' likely reaction; 'They saw that the camp had a lot of food, and surely they would stay.'
  • 不知中藏火箭火球等物也 - Reference to the hidden weapons; 'Not knowing that there were hidden rockets and fireballs.'
  • 彼方住歇，我使人由地道燃火线轰击 - Describing the execution of the plan; 'When they were resting, I sent people through the underground tunnels to ignite the fuse and attack.'
  • 贼已胆裂，外又促之，进退不得，有不降哉 - Explaining the outcome; 'The bandits were already terrified, and being pressed from the outside, they could neither advance nor retreat, so they had no choice but to surrender.'
  • 今以降人居外屯田，有益无损，是以日见富强 - Indicating the long-term strategy; 'Now using the surrendered people to farm outside, it benefits us without any harm, and thus our strength grows daily.'
  • 今富贵全矣，卿诚天人也 - Compliment to the speaker; 'Now we are wealthy and powerful, you truly are a heavenly being.'
  • 富不过百万，贵不过土司 - Realistic assessment of limits; 'Wealth cannot exceed a million, nobility cannot exceed that of a local chieftain.'
  • 卑卑者何足道哉 - Dismissive statement; 'Such small matters are not worth mentioning.'
  • 行将建大功于国家，膺天朝之高爵厚禄，方吐英雄之气耳 - Statement of future ambitions; 'We will soon achieve great deeds for the country, receive high ranks and generous rewards from the imperial court, and then truly unleash our heroic spirit.'
  • 君姑待之 - Polite suggestion to wait for the results; 'Wait and see.'

  • 二人入拜 - The two individuals enter and bow.

  • 左右献茶，和尚举杯谓二人曰 - The monk takes a cup of tea and speaks to the two individuals.

  • 命二人擎杯至座前 - The monk instructs the two individuals to bring the cups to the front.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 痘疮者 - Refers to smallpox, a disease characterized by pustules.
  • 父母欲火所结 - Metaphorical expression meaning that smallpox is caused by the intense desires of the parents.
  • 天一生水 - Classical concept from the *I Ching* suggesting that the first element created by heaven is water.
  • 肾先成火，毒伏其中 - Medical belief that the kidneys are the first organ to form in the fetus and that smallpox toxins are latent within them.
  • 感时气即发 - Means that the disease is triggered by seasonal changes or environmental factors.
  • 不茹荤酒 - Refers to abstaining from meat and alcohol.
  • 富贵之儿，殇于痘者多 - Statement that wealthy children are more likely to die from smallpox.
  • 贫贱之子，伤于痘者少 - Statement that poor children are less likely to die from smallpox.
  • 成丁婚配后出痘，成者甚鲜 - Means that smallpox occurring after marriage and childbearing is rare and often fatal.
  • 先天已伤，不能通达故也 - Medical belief that smallpox damages the body's vital energy, making it difficult to recover.
  • 阴亏甚矣 - Indicates a severe deficiency of yin (cooling and nourishing energy).
  • 必补托而后可 - Means that treatment must involve strengthening the body and supporting its functions.
  • 六味地黄加减 - Refers to a modified version of the *Liu Wei Di Huang Wan* (Six Flavor Rehmannia Pill), a traditional Chinese medicine formula.
  • 苍术、白芷熏屋 - Means using mugwort and angelica root to fumigate the house, a traditional method to purify the air.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 痘疮 - Smallpox, a highly contagious and often deadly disease that was prevalent in ancient China.
  • 六味地黄加减 - A well-known traditional Chinese medicine formula used to treat deficiencies in yin and yang.
  • 苍术、白芷 - Traditional herbs used in fumigation to purify the air and prevent disease.
  • 邻翁 - Refers to a neighbor, often an older man, who is knowledgeable about traditional practices.
  • 药肆 - A traditional Chinese pharmacy or medicine shop.
  • 兴旺药肆 - A successful and reputable pharmacy.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄大 - The name of the patient.
  • 六味地黄加减 - The name of the traditional Chinese medicine formula.
  • 苍术 - The name of the herb mugwort.
  • 白芷 - The name of the herb angelica root.
  • 药肆 - The name of the traditional Chinese pharmacy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鋪伙甚忙 - Indicates that the shop assistants are very busy.
  • 包藥 - Refers to the act of preparing or wrapping medicine.
  • 以藥方誤插朮、芷包上 - Indicates that the prescription was mistakenly placed on the package of herbs, specifically *mugwort* (朮) and *spikenard* (芷).
  • 翁不知也 - Indicates that the old man did not know about the mistake.
  • 至家，交有方者与黄妻，嘱速煎而进之 - Means the old man brought the herbs home and handed them over to someone knowledgeable and his wife, instructing them to boil and administer them quickly.
  • 黄大服后，即得安眠 - Indicates that after taking the herbs, Huang fell asleep immediately.
  • 翁夜生盆火，开包取朮、芷，则皆药料矣 - The old man lit a fire in the evening, opened the package, and found that it contained medicinal ingredients instead of the herbs he expected.
  • 急往叩门问之 - The old man rushed to knock on the door to ask what happened.
  • 黄妻曰：「已煎服半日，睡甚安，似大见效。」 - Huang's wife replied that she had been boiling and administering the herbs for half a day, and Huang was sleeping peacefully, seeming to be greatly improved.
  • 翁不敢言而回 - The old man did not dare to say anything and returned home.
  • 次早往视，一身痘花皆现，光明磊落，且索饭食，神气间如无病者 - The next morning, the old man went to see Huang, who had developed a rash all over his body, which looked clear and bright, and he was eating and seemed to be in good spirits.
  • 翁诧异，即代为延医复诊 - The old man was surprised and immediately asked a doctor to come for a follow-up examination.
  • 医曰：「已反逆回顺，非我重用地黄，不能如是神速也。今可不药收功矣。」 - The doctor said that the condition had reversed, and without the heavy use of *Rehmannia* (地黄), it would not have improved so quickly. Now, no further medication was needed.
  • 翁心中抱歉，朝夕在侧，殷懃服侍 - The old man felt sorry and stayed by Huang's side day and night, serving him with great care.
  • 果十朝痘尽回，痂渐脱落，壮健如初 - Indeed, after ten days, all the rashes had healed, the scabs gradually fell off, and Huang was as strong and healthy as before.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 药方 - Refers to a traditional Chinese medicine prescription.
  • 朮、芷 - *Mugwort* (朮) and *spikenard* (芷) are traditional Chinese herbs.
  • 地黄 - *Rehmannia* (地黄) is a common herb used in traditional Chinese medicine, known for its ability to nourish the blood and yin.
  • 痘花 - Refers to smallpox pustules, a common disease in ancient times.
  • 延医复诊 - Refers to calling a doctor for a follow-up examination, a common practice in traditional Chinese medicine.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 以药方误插朮、芷包上 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous but can be interpreted as the prescription being mistakenly placed on the package of herbs.
  • 翁夜生盆火，开包取朮、芷，则皆药料矣 - The sequence of actions is clear but could be read as a series of events leading to the discovery of the mistake.
  • 黄妻曰：「已煎服半日，睡甚安，似大见效。」 - The wife's response is a direct quote, indicating her observation and the perceived effectiveness of the treatment.
  • 医曰：「已反逆回顺，非我重用地黄，不能如是神速也。今可不药收功矣。」 - The doctor's explanation is a direct quote, providing insight into the medical reasoning behind the rapid improvement.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 巨室以千金延名医薛生白在邑 - A wealthy family invited the famous doctor Xue Shengbai to the county with a thousand gold pieces.
  • 二人执香跪门求诊 - Two people knelt at the door holding incense to seek medical treatment.
  • 生白悯其诚 - Xue Shengbai pitied their sincerity.
  • 脉甚和平，无疾何治 - The pulse is very peaceful, there is no illness, so why treat?
  • 翁始以疑质之 - The old man first questioned him with doubt.
  • 生白大笑曰 - Xue Shengbai laughed loudly and said.
  • 是矣，此必黄君祖功宗德，不应绝后，故有此误 - That's right, this must be due to Master Huang's ancestors' merits and virtues, should not be cut off from descendants, so there is this mistake.
  • 若服地黄汤，闷死久矣 - If he took the Rehmannia soup, he would have suffocated long ago.
  • 时医不知用药之活变，而执古方，冤死者不少 - Contemporary doctors do not know the flexibility in using medicine, but stick to ancient prescriptions, many innocent people die.
  • 彼意谓有夫妇，必有房事，亏损肾经 - They think there are married couples, there must be sexual intercourse, damage the kidney meridian.
  • 不知夫妇之事，天生天化，互相补益 - They do not know marital relations are naturally generated and transformed, mutually benefit each other.
  • 若以为有房事者必损，则世无八九十岁夫妇偕老之人，而鳏寡孤独，皆千万岁矣 - If they think sexual intercourse must cause damage, then there would be no couples aged eighty or ninety living together, and widowers, widows, orphans, and the elderly would all live for thousands of years.
  • 惟好色过度则伤，黄君一夫一妇，人伦之常，复有何损 - Only excessive lust causes harm, Master Huang has one husband and one wife, the normal human relationship, what harm could there be?
  • 其痘之郁而不发者，必平日尽力农田，长浸水中，深受湿气，遏不得达，是以痛楚 - The sores that are suppressed and not erupting, must be because he works hard in the fields every day, often immersed in water, deeply affected by dampness, blocked from reaching the surface, that is why it hurts.
  • 老翁所市术、芷必有数两，大剂投之，水气立散，有不畅茂条达者乎 - The old man must have bought some Atractylodes and Artemisia, several ounces, a large dose is given, the dampness immediately disperses, is there any reason for it not to flourish and be healthy?
  • 时医何能见及此 - How could contemporary doctors see this?
  • 我故曰：祖功宗德，鬼神所使也 - I therefore say, ancestral merits and virtues, are caused by spirits.
  • 且黄君脉大而长，不但痘不复发，且享寿无疑 - And Master Huang's pulse is strong and long, not only will the sores not recur, but he will surely enjoy a long life.

  • 乌稍蛇 - A specific type of snake, possibly known for its venomous nature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一路毛发脱落，日渐周身发痒 - Describes a progressive physical condition where hair falls out and the body becomes increasingly itchy; literal description of a disease process.
  • 未几癞已匝身，奄奄一息 - Describes the severe state of the disease where leprosy had spread all over the body and the patient was on the brink of death.
  • 及家，其次兄收之，虑其蔓延，锁于酒房下榻 - Upon arriving home, the younger brother took him in, fearing the disease would spread, and locked him up in the wine cellar.
  • 嫂氏哀之，使老媪给饮食 - The sister-in-law pitied him and sent an elderly woman to provide food and drink.
  • 先是介弟去后，女方知其事，乃与父母为难，誓不二天，必欲同死 - Before this, after the younger brother left, the woman learned about the situation, argued with her parents, swore never to remarry, and determined to die with him.
  • 其父母婉劝教戒，矢志不回，不得已，以实情告 - Her parents gently advised and warned her, but she remained steadfast in her resolve; reluctantly, they told her the truth.
  • 太守敬其节义，允为作札，遣送姑苏，为弟守节，来投嫂氏 - The magistrate respected her loyalty and virtue, agreed to write a letter of introduction, sent her to Suzhou to keep her promise to her brother, and come to her sister-in-law.
  • 嫂谓女曰：「叔病癞，已不起矣，莫如原舟遄返，以妹品貌，何患无好逑君子，何必恋此及泉人耶？」 - The sister-in-law said to the woman, 'Your uncle has leprosy and is on the brink of death; it would be better to return home quickly. With your beauty and virtue, why worry about not finding a good match? Why cling to someone who is already dead?'
  • 女泣曰：「妾故知之，不忍郎之独为癞鬼。且女身不可二天，来就死耳，非效于飞之乐也。」 - The woman cried and said, 'I already know; I cannot bear to see my husband alone as a leper. A woman cannot remarry; I came to die with him, not to enjoy life together.'
  • 嫂怜而敬之。送女入酒房，与介弟相抱而泣。女乃遣婢仆归复命。亲为其夫调养 - The sister-in-law pitied and respected her, sent the woman into the wine cellar, embraced her younger brother and wept, sent the servants back to report, and cared for her husband personally.

  • 癞 - Refers to leprosy, a serious and stigmatized disease in ancient times.
  • 太守 - Refers to a high-ranking official, often the governor of a prefecture.
  • 姑苏 - Refers to Suzhou, an important city in Jiangsu Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 于飞 - A metaphor for a happy married life, derived from the phrase '于飞之乐' (the joy of flying together).

Proper Nouns

  • 太守 - High-ranking official.
  • 姑苏 - Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 于飞 - Metaphor for a happy married life.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '贷亲友数十金，入都会试，独行至王家营，以三十金雇车' describes a series of actions leading up to the main event.
  • The parallel structure '两骡甚膘壮，车夫目立眉扬，挺胸凸肚，亦甚勇健' describes the coachman's physical appearance and demeanor.
  • The phrase '甫入山东境，车价支讫' indicates the timing of the payment of the fare.
  • The clause '宿第三站时，入陈君室坐，借十金，如不给不能前' describes the coachman's demand and Mr. Chen's refusal.
  • The rhetorical question '此地距都千有余里，安得多金济汝？' emphasizes the impracticality of the coachman's demand.
  • The conditional statement '果不能前，还我原价，另雇可也' offers a solution to the conflict.
  • The phrase '长装短卸，向有行规' introduces the established rules for long-distance travel.
  • The strong statement '休想毫厘也，我不惯与小人乘，请辞' shows the coachman's arrogance and disrespect.
  • The phrase '高声纷争，陈君泣下' describes the emotional impact of the conflict.
  • The detailed description '是室逼近上房，所寓亦会试新孝廉，大车五六辆，主仆十余人，方唤妓雅歌侑酒' sets the scene and provides context for the intervention.
  • The clause '闻攘攘声，使仆询问' shows the initial response to the commotion.
  • The phrase '我等算帐，何预汝事' indicates the coachman's initial refusal to engage with the inquiry.
  • The clause '陈乃实诉所苦' reveals the true nature of the problem.
  • The sequence '仆入主出，携陈君去，叙科分，同年也' describes the resolution and the discovery of a shared connection.
  • The clause '同行三孝廉，此君文姓，为主，谓陈曰' introduces the new characters and their relationship.
  • The statement '兄独行踽踽，宜为小人所侮' explains the coachman's behavior.
  • The statement '我闻车夫甚横，当教戒之' sets the stage for the intervention.
  • The question '汝何敢与客争' challenges the coachman's behavior.
  • The statement '小人贫困无聊，故为人仆，不过向客假数金耳，不意其肆炰烋也' provides the coachman's justification and the reason for the conflict.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 文孝廉 - Title given to scholars who passed the imperial examination, indicating a person of moral integrity and scholarly achievement.
  • 果为是 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if it is indeed so.'
  • 我假汝十金 - 'I will lend you ten gold coins,' where '假' (jia) means 'to lend' or 'to borrow.'
  • 明日随帮早行 - 'Tomorrow you will leave with the group early,' where '随帮' (sui bang) means 'to follow the group' or 'to travel with the group.'
  • 不许再有后言矣 - 'Do not allow any further complaints,' where '后言' (hou yan) means 'further complaints' or 'further words.'
  • 五鼓开行 - 'At the fifth drum, we set off,' where '五鼓' (wu gu) refers to the fifth drum beat, a traditional Chinese time signal indicating the start of the day.
  • 使陈君车在文前 - 'Make sure Chen Jun's carriage is ahead of Wen's,' where '陈君' (chen jun) is a respectful way to address Chen Jun, and '文' (wen) refers to Wen Xiaolian.
  • 车夫趋狭路疾驰 - 'The driver sped down a narrow road,' where '趋' (qu) means 'to rush' or 'to hurry,' and '疾驰' (ji chi) means 'to gallop' or 'to drive fast.'
  • 陈觉有异 - 'Chen noticed something unusual,' where '觉' (jue) means 'to notice' or 'to feel.'
  • 始则喝问 - 'At first, he shouted questions,' where '喝问' (he wen) means 'to shout questions' or 'to demand answers.'
  • 继以哀求 - 'Then he pleaded,' where '继' (ji) means 'then' or 'subsequently,' and '哀求' (ai qiu) means 'to plead' or 'to beg.'
  • 今日之事我为政 - 'Today's matter is under my control,' where '为政' (wei zheng) means 'to be in charge' or 'to have authority.'
  • 汝尚有同年友相助耶 - 'Do you still have fellow examinees to help you?' where '同年友' (tong nian you) means 'fellow examinees' or 'classmates.'
  • 文孝廉仆 - 'Wen Xiaolian's servant,' where '仆' (pu) means 'servant' or 'attendant.'
  • 是不怀好意 - 'This is not a good intention,' where '不怀好意' (bu huai hao yi) means 'not having good intentions.'
  • 疾跃出车 - 'He quickly leaped out of the carriage,' where '疾跃' (ji yue) means 'to leap quickly.'
  • 解骖 - 'Unhitched the horses,' where '解骖' (jie can) means 'to unhitch the horses.'
  • 乘健骡 - 'Rode a strong donkey,' where '健骡' (jian luo) means 'strong donkey.'
  • 不鞍而驰之 - 'Rode without a saddle,' where '不鞍' (bu an) means 'without a saddle,' and '驰之' (chi zhi) means 'to gallop it.'
  • 车夫有觉追者 - 'The driver realized someone was chasing,' where '觉' (jue) means 'to realize' or 'to notice,' and '追者' (zhui zhe) means 'chaser' or 'pursuer.'
  • 奋力鞭两骡 - 'Furiously whipped the two donkeys,' where '奋力' (fen li) means 'with great effort,' and '鞭两骡' (bian liang luo) means 'whipped the two donkeys.'

  • 我虽文士，而习武功 - Literally 'I am a scholar, but I practice martial arts,' indicating a dual identity.

  • 汝观我以两手倒挽五六套大车，可使逆行 - Means 'look at me using both hands to pull a few carts backward, making them go in reverse,' demonstrating a remarkable skill.

  • 咋舌 - Means 'to be speechless' or 'to gasp in amazement,' indicating astonishment.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 目开口张，不能言语 - Their eyes were open, their mouths wide, and they could not speak, indicating a state of unconsciousness or severe illness.

  • 我曾說以捕法 - I once said to catch it, where '捕法' (catching method) likely refers to a method of preparation or consumption.
  • 諒必捕而食之 - They must have caught and eaten it, indicating a likely sequence of events.
  • 未識九蒸九晒之制 - They did not know the method of nine steams and nine dries, where '九蒸九晒' (nine steams and nine dries) is a traditional method of processing herbs.
  • 又不知避忌 - They also did not know the taboos, indicating a lack of knowledge about proper usage.

  • 試以解毒開通之藥灌之 - They tried to administer detoxifying and opening drugs to them, where '開通' (opening) likely refers to restoring normal bodily functions.
  • 至七日，腫消人醒 - After seven days, the swelling subsided and they woke up, indicating recovery.

  • 連舉子女十餘 - She gave birth to over ten children, indicating a miraculous fertility.
  • 後皆壽一百五六十歲，無疾而終 - Later, they all lived to be over 150 years old and died without any illness, indicating a long and healthy life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 首烏 - Polygonum multiflorum, a traditional Chinese herb known for its medicinal properties, particularly for longevity and rejuvenation.
  • 九蒸九晒 - A traditional method of processing herbs, involving repeated steaming and drying, believed to enhance their medicinal properties.
  • 周甲 - A term used to describe someone who is 60 years old, as '周' (complete) and '甲' (the first of the ten heavenly stems) together symbolize a full cycle of 60 years.
  • 二八 - A term used to describe someone who is 16 years old, as '二' (two) and '八' (eight) together symbolize 16 years.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惜乎 - Expression of regret or pity, equivalent to 'alas' or 'how unfortunate.'
  • 得灵药而不知服食之法 - Literally 'having obtained the elixir but not knowing the method of consumption,' used to express a missed opportunity.
  • 仅以延龄 - Literally 'merely to extend life,' indicating a limited benefit.
  • 有负仙品 - Literally 'fall short of the status of an immortal,' suggesting a failure to live up to one's potential.
  • 或问制食之法 - Literally 'someone asks about the method of preparation,' where '制食' can also mean 'preparation' or 'processing.'
  • 曾考诸文献 - Literally 'I have examined the literature,' indicating a scholarly approach.
  • 柳木作桶 - Literally 'making a bucket from willow wood,' specifying the material and shape.
  • 承以砂甑 - Literally 'supported by a sand steamer,' describing the setup for cooking.
  • 用桑木火 - Literally 'using mulberry wood fire,' specifying the type of fire.
  • 先武后文 - Literally 'first vigorous, then gentle,' describing a cooking technique.
  • 九蒸九晒 - Literally 'nine steams and nine dries,' a traditional method of processing herbs.
  • 以竹刀剖作九顿食 - Literally 'using a bamboo knife to cut into nine servings,' indicating a specific method of preparation.
  • 可以绝粒入山矣 - Literally 'can abstain from food and enter the mountains,' suggesting a state of spiritual purity.
  • 徒虚语耳 - Literally 'just empty words,' suggesting that the advice is impractical or unrealistic.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 灵药 - Refers to a mythical or highly potent elixir, often associated with immortality in Chinese culture.
  • 仙品 - Refers to the status of an immortal, a person who has achieved a high level of spiritual attainment.
  • 柳木 - Willow wood, known for its flexibility and use in traditional crafts.
  • 砂甑 - A type of steamer made of sand, used for cooking and processing herbs.
  • 桑木火 - Fire made from mulberry wood, known for its quality and use in traditional cooking.
  • 竹刀 - A knife made from bamboo, often used in traditional Chinese medicine and cooking.
  • 九蒸九晒 - A traditional method of processing herbs, believed to enhance their medicinal properties.
  • 绝粒入山 - A practice of abstaining from food and entering the mountains, often associated with asceticism and spiritual cultivation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '惜乎！得灵药而不知服食之法，仅以延龄，无乃有负仙品' expresses regret and a sense of missed opportunity, followed by a question about whether the elixir's limited use falls short of its potential.
  • The sentence '或问制食之法，余曰曾考诸文献，当以柳木作桶，承以砂甑，用桑木火，先武后文，九蒸九晒，以竹刀剖作九顿食，可以绝粒入山矣' describes a detailed method of preparing the elixir, with each step carefully specified.
  • The sentence '或笑曰：能得者不能制，能制者必不能得，徒虚语耳' introduces a skeptical response, questioning the practicality of the method described.

  • 寓吕祖阁 - Means 'lodged at Lüzu Pavilion,' indicating where Zhang stayed.
  • 阁前树木茂盛 - Means 'the trees in front of the pavilion were lush and dense,' describing the environment.
  • 有亭纳凉甚适 - Means 'there was a pavilion where one could cool off very comfortably,' highlighting the pleasant setting.
  • 阁之左有大宅 - Means 'to the left of the pavilion was a large mansion,' describing the location of the mansion.
  • 系候补太守公馆 - Means 'it was the residence of a deputy magistrate,' indicating the status of the mansion.
  • 其仆从暇即来亭内坐谈 - Means 'his servants would come to the pavilion to chat during their leisure time,' describing the interaction between Zhang and the servants.
  • 以是识张 - Means 'and thus became acquainted with Zhang,' indicating how Zhang met the servants.
  • 数日间 - Means 'within a few days,' indicating a short period.
  • 闻太守盛怒 - Means 'heard that the magistrate was very angry,' describing the situation.
  • 鞭扑家人 - Means 'beating his family members,' indicating the severity of the magistrate's anger.
  • 骂詈无已 - Means 'continuously cursing and scolding,' describing the ongoing nature of the magistrate's anger.
  • 楚囚相对 - Means 'looking at each other like prisoners from Chu,' a metaphor for being in a dire and helpless situation.
  • 非复从前畅快矣 - Means 'no longer as carefree as before,' indicating a change in the servants' mood.
  • 张偶询之 - Means 'Zhang casually asked them,' describing how Zhang inquired about the situation.
  • 一仆告曰 - Means 'one servant replied,' indicating the servant's response.
  • 主人蜀之富族 - Means 'the master is a wealthy family from Shu,' providing background information about the magistrate.
  • 纳资得官 - Means 'obtained an official position by paying a fee,' indicating how the magistrate acquired his position.
  • 所好者精馔 - Means 'he likes fine cuisine,' describing the magistrate's interest in gourmet food.
  • 有得意庖人偕来 - Means 'he brought along a skilled cook,' indicating the presence of a talented chef.
  • 日前中暑而死 - Means 'recently died of heatstroke,' describing the cook's death.
  • 主命我辈觅人 - Means 'the master ordered us to find someone,' indicating the magistrate's command.
  • 不如意则挞举荐者 - Means 'if we don't find a suitable person, he will beat the one who recommended us,' describing the consequences of failure.
  • 连挞多人 - Means 'he has beaten many people,' indicating the severity of the situation.
  • 皆不敢引进矣 - Means 'none of them dared to recommend anyone,' describing the servants' reluctance.
  • 又詈我等无良心 - Means 'he also cursed us for lacking a conscience,' indicating the magistrate's harsh words.
  • 必欲饿死主人也 - Means 'he wants to starve the master to death,' describing the magistrate's extreme anger.
  • 故嚷嚷不绝 - Means 'so he continues to shout and complain,' indicating the ongoing nature of the situation.
  • 不意偌大郡城 - Means 'unexpectingly, in such a large city,' expressing surprise.
  • 寻不出一善庖者 - Means 'cannot find one skilled cook,' indicating the difficulty in finding a replacement.
  • 我辈命该绝矣 - Means 'our fate is sealed,' expressing the servants' despair.
  • 微哂曰 - Means 'smiled slightly and said,' describing Zhang's reaction.
  • 我以为有大事也 - Means 'I thought there was a major event,' indicating Zhang's initial assumption.
  • 故如此盛怒 - Means 'so he is so furious,' explaining the reason for the magistrate's anger.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鹾商 - Refers to salt merchants, a historically significant and often wealthy class in Chinese society.
  • 武林 - Refers to Wulin, an old name for Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 吕祖阁 - Refers to Lüzu Pavilion, a place associated with the Taoist deity Lü Dongbin, known for his wisdom and benevolence.
  • 候补太守 - Refers to a deputy magistrate, a lower-ranking official who is waiting for a higher position.
  • 蜀 - Refers to Sichuan Province, historically known for its wealth and cultural richness.
  • 纳资得官 - Refers to the practice of obtaining an official position by paying a fee, a common practice during the late Qing dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 张生 - Zhang, the main character.
  • 鹾商 - Salt merchant, referring to Zhang's father.
  • 武林 - Wulin, an old name for Hangzhou.
  • 吕祖阁 - Lüzu Pavilion, the place where Zhang stayed.
  • 候补太守 - Deputy magistrate, the official who is the magistrate's superior.
  • 蜀 - Sichuan, the province where the magistrate is from.
  • 庖人 - Cook, referring to the skilled chef.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 区区者 - A humble or modest way of referring to oneself or one's abilities, often translated as 'a humble person' or 'a small matter.'
  • 何地无之 - A phrase meaning 'there is no place without it,' indicating universality.
  • 诚不善觅耳 - A phrase meaning 'you are not good at finding it,' suggesting a lack of skill or ability.
  • 未识汝主果知味否 - A phrase meaning 'I do not know if your master truly appreciates the taste,' expressing uncertainty about someone's discernment.
  • 姑耐一顿皮鞭 - A phrase meaning 'just endure a beating,' indicating a willingness to accept punishment for a chance.
  • 我等姑耐一顿皮鞭，请试为之 - A phrase meaning 'we will just endure a beating, please try it,' showing a group's willingness to take a risk.
  • 夕飧进之 - A phrase meaning 'present it at the evening meal,' indicating the timing of the presentation.
  • 其香始升，不觉触鼻 - A phrase meaning 'the fragrance began to rise, unconsciously touching the nose,' describing the sensory experience.
  • 何来此味 - A phrase meaning 'where does this flavor come from,' expressing surprise or curiosity.
  • 试尝而甘之 - A phrase meaning 'try tasting it and find it sweet,' indicating a positive reaction.
  • 大啖饱食 - A phrase meaning 'eat heartily and become full,' describing a satisfying meal.
  • 我非庖人，因图馆寓此，行将归矣 - A phrase meaning 'I am not a cook, I am staying here temporarily, and I am about to leave,' indicating a temporary and voluntary situation.
  • 岂为人作厨下媪哉 - A phrase meaning 'how could I serve as a kitchen maid,' expressing a refusal to take on a menial role.
  • 汝主必欲留我，岁修三百金，亲来聘请，为司账房，兼督庖厨可也 - A phrase meaning 'your master must want to keep me, offering 300 gold coins annually, personally inviting me to be the accountant, and also overseeing the kitchen,' indicating a negotiation for a position.
  • 未几，太守得缺，张为司总，加修至千金 - A phrase meaning 'after a short time, the magistrate received a vacancy, and Zhang was appointed as the head, with the salary increased to 1,000 gold coins,' describing a promotion and salary increase.
  • 因此起家 - A phrase meaning 'thus establishing a family,' indicating the foundation of a successful career or business.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庖人 - Refers to a cook or chef.
  • 司账房 - Refers to an accountant or bookkeeper.
  • 督庖厨 - Refers to overseeing the kitchen or cooking staff.
  • 太守 - Refers to a magistrate or governor, a high-ranking official in ancient China.
  • 馆寓 - Refers to a temporary residence or lodging.
  • 岁修 - Refers to an annual salary or stipend.

Proper Nouns

  • 张 - A proper name, likely referring to a specific individual.
  • 太守 - A title, referring to a magistrate or governor.
  • 馆寓 - A term referring to a temporary residence or lodging.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無端发声 - To cry without any apparent reason.
  • 日夜不绝声 - To cry continuously day and night.
  • 闻者泪下 - Those who hear it shed tears.
  • 客来不绝 - Guests come without ceasing.
  • 内帏须终日哭 - The inner chambers must cry all day.
  • 主妇力不逮 - The mistress's strength is insufficient.
  • 或荐此妇代哭 - Or recommend this woman to cry in her place.
  • 吊者闻其哀甚 - Those who come to mourn hear her grief deeply.
  • 佥称主妇孝 - All praise the mistress for her filial piety.
  • 通邑无二人 - Throughout the city, there are no two of them.
  • 故鲜暇日 - Thus, she has few idle days.
  • 亦得小康 - And also manages to live comfortably.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南俗 - Refers to the customs and practices of the southern regions of China.
  • 富贵之家 - Refers to wealthy and noble families.
  • 丧吊 - Refers to funerals and the practice of offering condolences.
  • 内帏 - Refers to the inner chambers of a household, where women were typically confined and performed domestic duties, including mourning rituals.
  • 主妇 - Refers to the head of the household, usually the wife, who was responsible for managing the household and performing rituals, including mourning.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence introduces a woman who is skilled in crying and describes her ability to move others to tears without any apparent reason.
  • This sentence describes the custom in southern regions where wealthy families would have guests continuously during funerals, and the women in the inner chambers would cry all day.
  • This sentence explains that if the mistress cannot cry continuously, she might recommend this woman to cry on her behalf, as she can cry without ceasing day and night.
  • This sentence states that those who come to mourn are deeply moved by her crying and praise the mistress for her apparent filial piety.
  • This sentence indicates that families with funerals compete to invite her, and she is the only one of her kind in the city.
  • This sentence concludes that she has little free time but still manages to live comfortably.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人既不能上达 - Classical expression meaning 'since people cannot attain higher understanding.'
  • 必习一长一技 - Means 'one must acquire at least one skill or trade.'
  • 以为仰事俯育之资 - Means 'to provide for the support of one's parents and the upbringing of one's children.'
  • 妄想求财 - Means 'to foolishly seek wealth.'
  • 我不知渠何所藉 - Means 'I do not know what he relies on.'
  • 前三人，亦命应如此 - Means 'the first three people were also destined to be like this.'
  • 饱学秀才，埋没在蒙馆中者，不知凡几 - Means 'there are countless well-learned scholars who are buried in private schools.'
  • 更何足道哉 - Means 'how much more need it be mentioned?'
  • 误人不浅 - Means 'to mislead people greatly.'
  • 无所凭借而待命 - Means 'to wait for fate without any support.'
  • 天其雨金雨粟乎 - Means 'will heaven rain gold and grain?'
  • 励中人 - Means 'to encourage the average person.'
  • 袜匠，业甚兴隆 - Means 'a sock maker whose business was very prosperous.'
  • 晚年得子，爱如珍宝 - Means 'who had a son late in life and loved him like a treasure.'
  • 犹不使学事业 - Means 'still did not allow him to learn a profession.'
  • 有命存也 - Means 'it is due to fate.'
  • 大富造 - Means 'a great wealth maker.'
  • 行年三十，家资五百万 - Means 'at the age of thirty, his family wealth would be five million.'
  • 无出其右者 - Means 'there is none superior to this.'
  • 无忘发财之年 - Means 'do not forget the year of wealth.'
  • 益任其怠惰矣 - Means 'he became even more indulgent in his laziness.'
  • 完姻后 - Means 'after marrying off his son.'
  • 相继亡 - Means 'died one after another.'
  • 所遗袜肆，为其伙抵盗净尽 - Means 'the inherited sock shop was completely lost due to theft by his partners.'
  • 夫妇相携行乞 - Means 'the couple went begging together.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar who has passed the lowest level of the imperial examination.

  • 抵盗 - Refers to theft or embezzlement.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如此 - Common phrase meaning 'like this' or 'in this manner', used to refer to a previous statement or situation.

  • 应以贸易起家 - Formal expression meaning 'should have started a family through trade'.
  • 交招财利市二神 - Refers to the deities '招财' (attracting wealth) and '利市' (profit), who are invoked to bring prosperity.
  • 渐成家业 - Means 'gradually build up one's family wealth' or 'establish a family fortune'.
  • 三百六十行买卖 - Classical idiom referring to all trades and professions, literally 'the 360 trades of buying and selling'.
  • 误习文学 - Means 'mistakenly pursue literature' or 'erroneously study literature'.

  • 误习武 - Means 'mistakenly pursue martial arts' or 'erroneously study military skills'.
  • 转轮王 - The Wheel-Turning King, a Buddhist figure symbolizing universal sovereignty and justice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 招财利市二神 - Two deities associated with wealth and prosperity, commonly invoked in Chinese folk religion.
  • 文昌宫 - The Temple of Literature, dedicated to the deity of literature and education, Wen Chang.

Proper Nouns
