Idiomatic Expressions

  • 押至第十殿 - To押 to the tenth court, referring to being judged in the underworld, specifically the tenth court of judgment.
  • 判检簿曰 - To judge and inspect the record and say, referring to the act of reviewing one's deeds in the afterlife.
  • 有之 - A simple affirmative response meaning 'yes' or 'it is true.'
  • 因其人既不习文武，又不习商贾，无从给发 - Describes a person who has no skills in either literature or martial arts, nor in commerce, and thus cannot earn a living.
  • 饬交当方土地，埋藏其家，使掘地得之甚易 - Describes an action taken by the underworld to provide the person with wealth, but in a way that is too easy to find, implying a lack of effort.
  • 今犹未得，是土地之过也 - Now still not obtained, it is the fault of the local land god, indicating that the local land god failed to fulfill its duty.
  • 请追问之 - Please pursue the matter further, suggesting a request for accountability.
  • 领有此银 - To have this silver, referring to the wealth provided by the underworld.
  • 知其人已流落枯庙 - Knowing that the person has become destitute and living in an abandoned temple, indicating the person's current state of poverty.
  • 即以其银埋在庙阶之下 - To bury the silver under the temple steps, describing how the wealth was hidden.
  • 无奈其人从不动土，且未曾扫地 - Unfortunately, the person never digs the ground, and also never sweeps the floor, implying that the person does not make any effort to find the wealth.
  • 欲雨给之，恐其不知暂避，误伤性命 - To give it to him by rain, but fearing he would not know to temporarily avoid it, mistakenly injuring his life, suggesting that the underworld tried to provide the wealth indirectly but was concerned about the person's safety.
  • 原财奉缴，以脱小神之累也 - To return the original wealth and hand it over, to free the little god from trouble, indicating that the local land god is willing to return the wealth to avoid punishment.
  • 嗟乎，天下竟有如此怠惰之人，神亦不能福之，使其为人也，实害之也 - Alas, there are such lazy people in the world, even gods cannot bless them; if they were human, it would actually harm them, expressing disappointment and a warning about the consequences of laziness.
  • 然某前世之福泽尚在，无已，判作富贵家猫，眠锦绣而食膏粱，毋庸自力 - However, my good fortune from past lives still exists, so I decree him to be a cat in a wealthy family, sleeping on brocade and eating rich food, without needing to exert effort, describing a judgment that the person will be reincarnated as a pampered cat.
  • 且所见之财，亦千百万也 - Moreover, the wealth he sees will also be in the millions, emphasizing the abundance of wealth in the new life.
  • 故人而无能，不如为畜 - Therefore, if a person is incapable, it is better to be an animal, concluding with a moral lesson about the value of effort and capability.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 第十殿 - Refers to the tenth court of judgment in the Chinese underworld, where final judgments are made based on one's deeds in life.
  • 判检簿 - Refers to the records of one's deeds in the afterlife, which are reviewed by judges.
  • 土地 - Refers to the local land god, a deity responsible for the well-being of a specific area.
  • 富贵家猫 - Refers to a pampered cat in a wealthy family, symbolizing a life of luxury without the need for effort.
  • 眠锦绣而食膏粱 - Describes a life of luxury, with '锦' (brocade) and '膏粱' (rich food) symbolizing high-quality materials and food.

Proper Nouns

  • 第十殿 - The tenth court of judgment in the Chinese underworld.
  • 王 - Refers to the judge or ruler in the underworld.
  • 土地 - The local land god.
  • 富贵家猫 - A pampered cat in a wealthy family.
  • 锦 - Brocade, a type of luxurious fabric.
  • 膏粱 - Rich food, symbolizing a luxurious diet.

[173]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 六壬神课 - A form of Chinese divination, specifically a branch of the 'Three Divinations,' which includes the 'Yi Jing,' 'Taixuanjing,' and 'Lixiang.' '六壬' (Liu Ren) refers to the six celestial stems used in the divination.
  • 吾乡张君，鹾商子也 - '吾乡' means 'my hometown,' '张君' is 'Mr. Zhang,' and '鹾商子' means 'son of a salt merchant.' '鹾' (cuó) is an archaic term for salt.
  • 幼多病，故不甚读书 - '幼多病' means 'was often ill as a child,' and '故不甚读书' means 'therefore did not read much.'
  • 敏甚，其慧解书旨，皆能领悟 - '敏甚' means 'very quick-witted,' '慧解书旨' means 'understanding the essence of books,' and '皆能领悟' means 'all could comprehend.'
  • 及冠，自觉气禀甚弱，不愿婚娶 - '及冠' means 'upon reaching adulthood,' '自觉气禀甚弱' means 'felt his constitution was very weak,' and '不愿婚娶' means 'did not wish to marry.'
  • 一日，在朋友案头见《大六壬》书，悦之，求教 - '一日' means 'one day,' '案头' means 'desk,' '悦之' means 'was delighted by it,' and '求教' means 'asked to learn.'
  • 友曰：「我虽知此，不甚精。」遂以大略指示之 - '我虽知此，不甚精' means 'I know this, but not very well,' and '遂以大略指示之' means 'so he roughly explained it to him.'
  • 张携归学习，不忍释手，复购求他本，以为揣摹 - '携归学习' means 'took it back to study,' '不忍释手' means 'could not let go of it,' '复购求他本' means 'bought other copies,' and '以为揣摹' means 'to study and emulate.'
  • 闻有秘本，不惜重价，不畏远道，务罗而致之 - '闻有秘本' means 'heard of secret editions,' '不惜重价' means 'did not hesitate to pay high prices,' '不畏远道' means 'did not fear long journeys,' and '务罗而致之' means 'determined to obtain them.'
  • 不售者，亲往手录 - '不售者' means 'those not for sale,' and '亲往手录' means 'personally went to copy them by hand.'
  • 是以其书盈室，多人所不经见者 - '是以' means 'therefore,' '其书盈室' means 'his books filled his room,' and '多人所不经见者' means 'many of which were unseen by others.'
  • 进束书不观，亦不肯为人决事 - '进束书不观' means 'no longer looked at books,' and '亦不肯为人决事' means 'also refused to make decisions for others.'
  • 有时自露其机，则无不中 - '有时自露其机' means 'sometimes revealed his insights,' and '则无不中' means 'and they were always accurate.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 六壬神课 - A traditional Chinese divination method that uses the six celestial stems to predict future events.
  • 鹾商 - A salt merchant, a significant economic class in ancient China due to the state monopoly on salt.
  • 及冠 - Reaching adulthood, traditionally marked by a coming-of-age ceremony.
  • 《大六壬》 - A book on the practice of Liu Ren divination, which is considered one of the most complex and accurate forms of Chinese divination.
  • 秘本 - Secret or rare editions of books, often containing unique or esoteric knowledge.
  • 道不远人 - A philosophical concept suggesting that the way or truth is accessible to everyone, not just scholars or the elite.
  • 束书不观 - A reference to the Confucian ideal of putting away books after gaining wisdom, implying a deeper understanding beyond mere textual knowledge.
  • 决事 - Making decisions, often in the context of divination or advisory roles.
  • 兄嫂 - Brother and sister-in-law, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 借寓 - Temporarily staying at someone else's home, often due to a need for shelter or safety.
  • 犹可及也 - Still in time, emphasizing the urgency of the situation.

Proper Nouns

  • 六壬神课 - Liu Ren divination.
  • 鹾商 - Salt merchant.
  • 《大六壬》 - A book on Liu Ren divination.
  • 秘本 - Secret or rare editions of books.
  • 道 - The way or truth.
  • 束书 - Books.
  • 决事 - Decisions.
  • 兄嫂 - Brother and sister-in-law.
  • 借寓 - Temporary stay.
  • 犹可及也 - Still in time.

Idiomatic Expressions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '我固不忍舍汝，但娶妻为子也' introduces the speaker's reluctance to part with someone but justifies it by the need for a wife to have children.
  • The direct question '汝能生育乎' follows logically from the previous statement.
  • The compound sentence '无余资再娶，不误我后嗣耶' expresses the financial constraint and the concern for the family line.
  • The sentence '毋恐，我有祖传符箓，能看香治病' provides a reassurance and introduces the speaker's magical abilities.
  • The complex sentence '尔倩人绘女仙像供养，我将有仙人附体，治病神效' outlines a ritual and its expected outcome.
  • The sentence '传播人知，业必兴隆' links the dissemination of knowledge to the success of the business.
  • The conditional sentence '得财后，任置妾媵，不尔禁也' offers a reward for success and grants permission.
  • The sentence '为我谋则善矣' acknowledges the benefit of the plan.
  • The rhetorical question '以男子身而为此，何能忍乎' expresses disbelief or skepticism about the act.
  • The phrase '益媚妩之曰' describes a change in demeanor and introduces a seductive tone.
  • The sentence '始虽楚而后乐，恐天下男子知此味' describes a transformation in experience and expresses concern about widespread knowledge.
  • The sentence '世间甘为此者，非我一人也' indicates that others share the same experience.
  • The sentence '如不信，请尝试之' encourages the other party to experience it firsthand.
  • The sentence '闺中事，外人不知，无妨互相为乐耶' emphasizes secrecy and suggests mutual pleasure.

  • 入室突拥而抚其下体 - Means 'he suddenly rushed into the room, embraced her, and touched her lower body,' describing the official's aggressive behavior.
  • 出其不意，不及掩饰 - Means 'taking her by surprise, she couldn't hide it,' indicating the fairy's inability to conceal her true nature.
  • 居然伟男子也 - Means 'turning out to be a man of great strength,' revealing the fairy's true identity.
  • 缚而讯之 - Means 'tying her up and interrogating her,' describing the official's actions after discovering the fairy's true nature.
  • 邢哀求包容，愿任鸡奸而多与之贿 - Means 'Xing pleaded for leniency, willing to submit to sodomy and offer more bribes,' indicating the fairy's desperate plea.
  • 村中不乏少艾妇女，非亲即故 - Means 'there are plenty of young and beautiful women in the village, none of whom are related to him,' highlighting the official's justification for his actions.
  • 容汝在此，皆不得作完人矣 - Means 'if I let you stay here, I won't be a complete man,' indicating the official's moral justification.
  • 且我获妖人，官赏必厚 - Means 'besides, capturing a demon will bring me a generous reward,' indicating the official's motivation.
  • 自贻伊戚耶 - Means 'isn't that self-inflicted trouble?' indicating the official's self-interest.
  • 并获刘六，送坊转入秋部 - Means 'along with Liu Liu, he sent them to the judicial department,' describing the official's actions.
  • 鞠实，于左道惑人本罪上，加重问拟缳首，即行正法 - Means 'after investigation, he was found guilty of using witchcraft to deceive people, and was sentenced to be hanged,' indicating the legal consequences.
  • 刘六照为从例，刺配黑龙江，给索伦达呼尔为奴 - Means 'Liu Liu was punished according to the law, exiled to Heilongjiang and given to the Soren Dahu'er tribe as a slave,' indicating Liu Liu's punishment.
  • 此嘉庆十二年四月案 - Means 'this case occurred in the fourth month of the twelfth year of Jiaqing,' providing the historical context.
  • 有友任刑曹者，录出原供如此 - Means 'a friend who served as a judicial official recorded the original confession like this,' indicating the source of the information.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 岂有场外解元哉？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'How could there be a top scholar outside the examination hall?'
  • 非汝误我而谁？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Who else could have made a mistake if not you?'
  • 无哗，使有人送渠入场，不得发解，汝认何罚？ - Command and question meaning 'Silence! Let someone escort him into the hall, and if he does not pass, what punishment do you accept?'
  • 挖我眸子去。 - Threat meaning 'Take out my eyes.'
  • 言出无悔。 - Statement meaning 'Once spoken, there is no turning back.'
  • 即取案上纸笔书条，钤以身佩小玺，命卫士送去。 - Description of actions meaning 'He immediately took paper and pen from the desk, wrote a note, stamped it with his small seal, and ordered the guards to deliver it.'
  • 半月后，来验汝言也。 - Statement meaning 'In half a month, come to verify your words.'
  • 唯唯而遁。 - Description of actions meaning 'He nodded and fled.'
  • 当卫士送士人至场外，传鼓称圣旨降，门官揭封去锁，内即传点开门，偕士人直入。 - Description of events meaning 'When the guards escorted the scholar to the entrance, they beat the drum and announced the emperor's decree, the gatekeeper broke the seal and unlocked the door, and they immediately opened the door and let the scholar enter together.'
  • 至公堂，知贡举各官跪接，卫士面南宣读毕，即去复命。 - Description of events meaning 'They arrived at the main hall, where the officials responsible for the examination knelt to greet him, the guards facing south read out the decree, and then left to report back.'
  • 御史捡卷，恭送士人入号。 - Description of events meaning 'The censor checked the examination papers and respectfully escorted the scholar into his examination room.'
  • 内外哄传皇帝送朋友进场，主考亦留心阅卷，见其文亦尚稳妥，与各官公议皆曰：「此公既为当今识拔，不可作第二人，以拂上意。」 - Description of events and opinions meaning 'Inside and outside, people were gossiping that the emperor had sent a friend to take the exam, and the chief examiner also paid attention to the papers, finding them quite sound, and after discussing with the other officials, they all said, 'Since this gentleman has been recognized by the emperor, we cannot rank him second, as it would go against the emperor's wishes.''
  • 竟中解元。 - Statement meaning 'He was eventually ranked first.'
  • 试官复命，力奖其文才出众，贺帝得人。 - Description of events meaning 'The examiners reported back, praising his exceptional talent, and congratulated the emperor on finding a good person.'
  • 帝大笑曰：「命也，命也。」 - Statement meaning 'The emperor laughed and said, 'It is fate, it is fate.'
  • 盖帝本欲使入场而不取，以难相士。 - Statement meaning 'The emperor originally wanted to let him enter but not pass, to challenge the fortune-teller.'
  • 因豹房新得美人，恋色而忘后命。 - Statement meaning 'Because he had recently obtained a new beauty in the tiger den, he was so infatuated that he forgot his later command.'
  • 今竟发解，益觉相士可异。 - Statement meaning 'Now that he has passed, the fortune-teller seems even more mysterious.'
  • 着锦衣卫追寻，杳不知其所之矣。 - Statement meaning 'He ordered the Imperial Guard to search for him, but he vanished without a trace.'

  • 卤簿之盛，彩舆之华 - The grand procession and the splendid decorated carriage. '卤簿' (halberd array) refers to the ceremonial procession, and '彩舆' (decorated carriage) refers to the ornate vehicle.
  • 光耀夺目 - A vivid description meaning 'shining brightly and dazzling the eyes.'

  • 衣甲执杵，昂昂而来 - Wearing armor and holding a staff, coming with a proud demeanor.

  • 僧命以参苓作糜进之 - Means 'the monk ordered to prepare a porridge with ginseng and poria to feed the patient.'
  • 旬日痊可 - Means 'in ten days, the patient fully recovered.'
  • 心悦诚服 - Means 'heartily convinced and sincerely convinced.'
  • 告以真姓名而求益 - Means 'told his real name and asked for more.'
  • 念其虚心向往 - Means 'thinking of his sincere desire to learn.'
  • 与一册而遣之 - Means 'gave him a book and sent him away.'
  • 自是天士学益进，无棘手之症矣 - Means 'from then on, Tian Shi's learning advanced further, and he had no difficult cases.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 僧 - Refers to a Buddhist monk, indicating a religious or spiritual figure.
  • 天士 - Refers to a doctor named Tian Shi, indicating a medical practitioner.
  • 白信 - Refers to white arsenic, a traditional Chinese medicine ingredient.
  • 砒霜 - Refers to arsenic, a toxic substance used in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 虫蛊 - Refers to a parasite infection, a common ailment in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 参苓 - Refers to ginseng and poria, traditional Chinese medicinal herbs.
  • 糜 - Refers to porridge, a common food in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 旬日 - Refers to ten days, indicating a short period of time.
  • 棘手之症 - Refers to difficult cases, indicating challenging medical conditions.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 醫道至葉天士，已成名手 - Means 'The art of medicine, by the time it reached Ye Tianshi, had already become a master.'
  • 猶耻不及人，而精益求精 - Means 'Yet he still felt ashamed of not being able to surpass others, and thus strived for perfection.'
  • 彼后生小子 - Refers to young and inexperienced people.
  • 不过读得《脉诀》、《本草》 - Means 'who have only read *Mai Jue* (Pulse Decisions) and *Ben Cao* (Herbal Medicine).'
  • 居然吾道是在 - Means 'and arrogantly claim that they understand the true path.'
  • 大胆行医，人命其何堪哉 - Means 'boldly practicing medicine, how can they bear the weight of human lives?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 津门档子班主 - Refers to the head of a performing troupe in Tianjin.
  • 锦衣绣裳天使 - Refers to a heavenly messenger dressed in splendid attire, symbolizing divine intervention.

[200]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 回首曰 - Classical phrase meaning 'turned around and said,' indicating a sudden or deliberate shift in attention.
  • 貴人在此耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is a distinguished person here?' used to express surprise or recognition.
  • 速回登州 - Means 'hurry back to Dengzhou,' with '速' emphasizing urgency.
  • 小神引道西行 - Means 'the little deity leads the way westward,' with '引道' meaning 'to lead the way.'
  • 自有善士相遇 - Means 'there will be good people who will meet,' with '善士' referring to virtuous or kind individuals.
  • 班主疫數内人也 - Means 'the master of the troupe is among those who are ill,' with '疫數内人' referring to people affected by an epidemic.
  • 自命不保 - Means 'cannot even protect oneself,' indicating a state of helplessness or vulnerability.
  • 何能追捕 - Means 'how could they be pursued or captured,' expressing impossibility.
  • 放心前去 - Means 'go ahead with peace of mind,' encouraging confidence and reassurance.
  • 潛起出探 - Means 'crept up and went out to investigate,' with '潛起' indicating stealthy movement.
  • 果見老翁執拂前引 - Means 'indeed saw an old man holding a whisk leading the way,' with '執拂' referring to holding a whisk, a symbol of authority or guidance.
  • 隨之逾牆，復逾城 - Means 'followed him over the wall and then over the city wall,' with '逾' meaning 'to cross over.'
  • 甚輕捷 - Means 'very agile,' describing the ease of movement.
  • 力竭不能前 - Means 'exhausted and could not go further,' indicating physical fatigue.
  • 卧于巨室大门下 - Means 'lay down at the gate of a large house,' with '巨室' referring to a large house or mansion.
  • 未幾，一翁出 - Means 'not long after, an old man came out,' with '未幾' indicating a short period of time.
  • 見童子倦眠 - Means 'saw the child tiredly sleeping,' with '倦眠' indicating tiredness while sleeping.
  • 貌甚清健 - Means 'looking very healthy and vigorous,' describing the child's appearance.
  • 心爱怜之 - Means 'was moved to pity him,' with '爱怜' indicating compassion or affection.
  • 叩其所自 - Means 'asked where he came from,' with '叩' meaning 'to ask' and '所自' meaning 'where he came from.'
  • 跪而啼曰 - Means 'knelt and wept, saying,' with '跪' meaning 'to kneel' and '啼' meaning 'to weep.'
  • 小人张姓 - Means 'I am a man named Zhang,' with '小人' a humble self-reference.
  • 天津人 - Means 'from Tianjin,' indicating the child's origin.
  • 父母俱殁 - Means 'both parents have passed away,' with '俱殁' indicating both parents are deceased.
  • 被堂兄欲卖充档班 - Means 'was to be sold by my elder brother to join a troupe,' with '欲卖' meaning 'wanted to sell' and '充档班' meaning 'to join a troupe.'
  • 畏惧行丐至此 - Means 'out of fear, I fled and begged my way here,' with '畏惧行丐' indicating fleeing due to fear and begging as a result.
  • 如长者见怜 - Means 'if the honorable person shows compassion,' with '见怜' meaning 'shows compassion.'
  • 仆役自愿也 - Means 'I am willing to be a servant,' with '仆役' meaning 'servant' and '自愿' meaning 'willingly.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 登州 - Refers to Dengzhou, an ancient city in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 小神 - Refers to a minor deity or spirit, often associated with local folklore or religious beliefs.
  • 善士 - Refers to virtuous or kind individuals, often used in Confucian contexts to describe morally upright people.
  • 班主 - Refers to the master of a troupe or group, often in the context of performing arts.
  • 疫数内人 - Refers to people affected by an epidemic, indicating a state of illness or suffering.
  • 巨室 - Refers to a large house or mansion, often associated with wealth and status.
  • 长者 - Refers to an honorable or respected elder, often used to show respect in Confucian society.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence introduces a sudden shift in attention and a command, which must be read as a direct speech from the old man.
  • This sentence is a conditional statement, implying that the old man will lead the way westward and there will be good people who will meet.
  • This sentence is a rhetorical question, emphasizing the impossibility of pursuing or capturing the master of the troupe.
  • This sentence is a reassurance, encouraging the listener to go ahead with peace of mind.
  • This sentence describes a sequence of actions, with the child creeping out to investigate and seeing the old man leading the way.
  • This sentence describes a series of actions, with the child following the old man over the wall and then over the city wall, emphasizing the agility of the movements.
  • This sentence describes the child's exhaustion and subsequent action of lying down at the gate of a large house.
  • This sentence describes a sequence of events, with an old man coming out and seeing the child tiredly sleeping, feeling compassion for him.
  • This sentence describes the child's response to the old man's question, detailing his background and current situation.

Proper Nouns

  • 登州 - The place name Dengzhou.
  • 天津 - The place name Tianjin.

[201]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兒與老夫同姓 - Formal way of indicating kinship by sharing the same surname.
  • 既有志不愿作档班 - Having already resolved not to become a servant, where '档班' refers to a low-ranking servant.
  • 岂可为僮仆 - How could he be made a servant? Expressing strong disapproval.
  • 速从我来，有以处子 - Come quickly with me, I have a plan for you, where '有以处子' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'I have a way to deal with you.'
  • 此儿有志向上，后必有为 - This child is ambitious and will surely achieve something in the future, a common phrase used to praise a child's potential.
  • 此人来历不明，且已行丐 - This person's background is unclear, and he has already been begging, indicating suspicion and concern.
  • 儿不愿有此下贱子也 - I do not want to have such a lowly child, expressing disdain.
  • 汝不欲耶，我子之，汝其弟之 - If you do not want him, I will adopt him, and you will be his brother, a formal way of offering adoption.
  • 乃引入室，见其爱妾曰 - He then brought him into the room and introduced him to his beloved concubine, describing a formal introduction.
  • 汝素日以无子为戚，今为汝得一佳儿矣 - You have always been troubled by not having a son, now I have found you a fine son, addressing a long-standing concern.
  • 命张拜为亲母，妾乐而抚之 - He ordered him to kneel and address her as his mother, and the concubine happily took care of him, describing a formal adoption ceremony.
  • 翁使入学读书，既勤且敏，五年而学成 - The old man sent him to school to study, and he was both diligent and quick-witted, completing his studies in five years, praising the child's academic success.
  • 文笔清灵，一试入泮，年仅十七 - His writing was clear and elegant, and he passed the imperial examination at the age of seventeen, highlighting his literary talent.
  • 翁为之娶妇分家，亲子得其六，螟蛉得其四 - The old man arranged for him to marry and set up a household, giving him six of his own children and four adopted ones, describing the distribution of property.
  • 乃兄敢怒而不敢言 - His elder brother dared to be angry but did not dare to speak out, reflecting the hierarchical nature of family relationships.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 同姓 - Sharing the same surname, a sign of familial connection in Chinese culture.
  • 档班 - A low-ranking servant, reflecting the social hierarchy of the time.
  • 僮仆 - Servants, indicating a lower social status.
  • 行丐 - Begging, a desperate act often associated with poverty and social stigma.
  • 下贱子 - A derogatory term for a lowly child, reflecting the social values of the time.
  • 爱妾 - A beloved concubine, indicating a secondary wife in a polygamous household.
  • 无子为戚 - Being troubled by not having a son, a common concern in traditional Chinese families due to the importance of male heirs.
  • 入泮 - Passing the imperial examination, a significant achievement that qualified one for government service.
  • 分家 - Setting up a separate household, often accompanied by the division of property.
  • 螟蛉 - Adopted children, often referred to as 'foster children' in modern terms.
  • 敢怒而不敢言 - Daring to be angry but not daring to speak out, reflecting the hierarchical nature of family relationships.

Proper Nouns

  • 翁 - The old man, a generic term for an elderly male.
  • 老夫 - An older man, often used as a self-reference by an elderly male.
  • 张 - The child's name, used in the context of the adoption ceremony.
  • 妾 - The concubine, a secondary wife in a polygamous household.
  • 入泮 - Passing the imperial examination, a significant achievement in traditional Chinese society.
  • 分家 - Setting up a separate household, often accompanied by the division of property.
  • 螟蛉 - Adopted children, often referred to as 'foster children' in modern terms.

[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未幾 - Temporal expression meaning 'not long after' or 'soon.'
  • 以異姓亂宗告逐 - Legal or familial term indicating that someone was accused of disrupting the family line by marrying into another clan and was subsequently expelled.
  • 幸父黨及張之同學諸生 - Indicates that the father's relatives and Zhang's fellow students came to Zhang's aid.
  • 辩非异姓，系其父爱继 - Suggests that they argued that the accused was not of a different surname and was actually the father's beloved adopted son.
  • 官怒其兄曰 - Indicates that the official became angry with Zhang's brother.
  • 汝父尸骨未寒，即欲逐其爱子，以不孝论 - A harsh rebuke, implying that the brother's actions are akin to filial impiety.
  • 欲重责之 - Indicates that the official wanted to severely punish the brother.
  • 张为哀求 - Indicates that Zhang pleaded on behalf of his brother.
  • 官以张情，断令各安其业 - Indicates that the official, considering Zhang's plea, decided to let everyone return to their respective occupations.
  • 张知日久必不见容，随携受分之物，奉嗣母入都 - Indicates that Zhang realized he would eventually be expelled and thus took what was given to him and accompanied his stepmother to the capital.
  • 时前明中叶，大珰王振用事 - Indicates that during the middle period of the previous Ming dynasty, the eunuch Wang Zhen held power.
  • 张以赂结 - Indicates that Zhang bribed Wang Zhen.
  • 珰谕张，使以张名贡入成均 - Indicates that Wang Zhen instructed Zhang to enter the Imperial Academy under his name.
  • 肄业期满，选得中州某县丞 - Indicates that after completing his studies, Zhang was selected as a county magistrate in a certain county in Central China.
  • 益媚事珰，不十年，官至廉访使 - Indicates that Zhang further flattered Wang Zhen and within ten years rose to the position of Censorate Inspector.
  • 值土木之变，王振死，失所依，忆昔土地奏臬使之言，知官止于是矣 - During the Tumu Crisis, Wang Zhen died, leaving Zhang without support. Zhang recalled the words of the land official who had advised him about the limits of his position.
  • 引疾归，置业津门，优游林下，又十余年而卒 - Zhang feigned illness to return home, established a residence in Tianjin, lived a leisurely life in retirement, and passed away after more than ten years.
  • 芗厈曰 - Introduces a commentary by Xiang Yan, likely a scholar or commentator.
  • 惜乎，张廉访之为人，虽脱其身，究不能脱档子气 - Expresses regret that despite Zhang's rise, he could not escape the influence of his lowly origins.
  • 故媚事大珰，以奔竞功名 - Indicates that Zhang flattered the eunuch to achieve fame and fortune.
  • 若有贤父兄教之，得为正人君子，既命应廉访，终必至此地位，益为后人所景仰，岂仅为档子出色耶 - Suggests that if Zhang had been taught by virtuous parents and brothers, he could have become a virtuous and upright person, and his eventual rise to the position of Censorate Inspector would have been even more admirable.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 前明中叶 - Refers to the middle period of the Ming dynasty.
  • 大珰王振 - Refers to Wang Zhen, a powerful eunuch during the Ming dynasty.
  • 成均 - Refers to the Imperial Academy, a prestigious educational institution in ancient China.
  • 土木之变 - Refers to the Tumu Crisis, a significant event in the Ming dynasty where the emperor was captured by the Mongols.
  • 廉访使 - Refers to the position of Censorate Inspector, a high-ranking official responsible for overseeing local administration and investigating corruption.
  • 档子 - Refers to a low social status or background, often associated with commoners or those of humble origins.

  • 土木之变 - The Tumu Crisis.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少君 - A respectful title for a young man, often used in Classical Chinese to denote a young scholar or gentleman.
  • 能詩文而未遇 - Literally means 'capable of poetry and prose but not yet recognized,' indicating that the young man has talent but has not yet achieved fame or success.
  • 翩翩美少年也 - Describes the young man as a graceful and handsome youth.
  • 科甲铨得四川县令 - Refers to the young man's father obtaining a county magistrate position in Sichuan through the imperial examination system.
  • 羊肠阪 - A term for a winding and difficult mountain pass, often used metaphorically to describe a challenging journey.
  • 马逸 - Means 'the horse ran away,' indicating an unexpected and dangerous situation.
  • 颠落崖下 - Describes the young man falling off a cliff.
  • 身糜而魂出 - Literally means 'body is destroyed and soul departs,' indicating a near-death experience.
  • 随风飘荡 - Means 'drifting with the wind,' suggesting a state of being lost or disoriented.
  • 瞬息数千里 - Means 'travelling thousands of miles in an instant,' indicating a rapid and vast journey.
  • 求止不得 - Means 'unable to find a place to rest,' suggesting a sense of aimlessness.
  • 初死男尸壳中 - Literally means 'in the shell of a recently deceased male body,' indicating that the young man's soul entered the body of a recently deceased man.
  • 大叫曰 - Means 'shouted loudly,' indicating a sudden and loud reaction.
  • 跌死我也 - Means 'I fell and died,' expressing the young man's realization of his near-death experience.
  • 皆止哭曰 - Means 'all stopped crying and said,' indicating a collective reaction of the people around him.
  • 苏矣 - Means 'he has revived,' indicating that the young man has come back to life.
  • 气绝逾日 - Means 'breath had ceased for more than a day,' indicating that the young man was believed to be dead for a significant period.
  • 气暖身和 - Means 'warm breath and harmonious body,' indicating that the young man's vital signs have returned.
  • 一家庆幸 - Means 'the whole family rejoiced,' indicating a collective sense of relief and happiness.
  • 频闻欢乐声 - Means 'frequently heard joyful sounds,' indicating a lively and happy atmosphere.
  • 汝何人，敢儿我 - Means 'Who are you, dare to call me your son?' indicating the young man's confusion and disbelief.
  • 渠虽少苏，神尚未完 - Means 'He has slightly revived, but his spirit is not yet fully restored,' indicating that the young man is still in a state of confusion and disorientation.
  • 此汝母 - Means 'this is your mother,' indicating the identity of the woman speaking to the young man.
  • 此汝妻 - Means 'this is your wife,' indicating the identity of the woman next to the young man.
  • 此汝子 - Means 'this is your son,' indicating the identity of the child next to the young man.
  • 皆识否 - Means 'do you recognize them all?' indicating the young man's confusion about his new surroundings.
  • 谬甚！谬甚！ - Means 'How absurd! How absurd!' indicating the young man's disbelief and frustration.
  • 我某公子，随父莅任 - Means 'I am the son of someone, traveling with my father to take up his post,' indicating the young man's attempt to assert his true identity.
  • 行蜀道上，堕马 - Means 'traveling on the road to Sichuan, I fell off my horse,' indicating the young man's recollection of the incident that led to his near-death experience.
  • 被风吹至此 - Means 'blown by the wind to this place,' indicating the young man's belief that he was transported to this location by supernatural means.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 并未娶妻，焉得有子 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Since he has not married, how could he have a son?' used to challenge the claim of having a child.
  • 诰命孺人 - Formal title given to a woman of high status, often a mother of a high-ranking official.
  • 村妪何得冒认 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How could a village woman claim such a title?' used to challenge the credibility of the claimant.
  • 休呓语 - Command to stop talking nonsense or delirious talk.
  • 可以镜自照 - Offer to look in a mirror to verify one's identity.
  • 四十余岁之麻胡也 - Description of a person in their forties with a disheveled appearance.
  • 扑镜大哭 - Vivid description of someone hitting the mirror and crying.
  • 还我本来面目 - Plea to restore one's original appearance.
  • 不愿生矣 - Statement expressing a desire to die rather than live.
  • 粲然皆笑 - Description of people laughing heartily.
  • 初醒，神尚模糊 - Description of someone just waking up and still feeling disoriented.
  • 静养数日，自然复原 - Suggestion to rest for a few days to recover naturally.
  • 绝粮三四日 - Description of being without food for three to four days.
  • 食槐皮野菜 - Description of eating wild vegetables and tree bark.
  • 忍耻向邻人乞得此饼 - Description of enduring shame to beg for food from neighbors.
  • 大人情 - Polite term meaning 'a gesture of goodwill' or 'a kind act.'
  • 犹以为不足耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Do you still think it's not enough?'
  • 大声叱之出 - Command to shout at someone to leave.
  • 垂首丧气 - Description of someone hanging their head in dejection.
  • 饥肠作辘辘声 - Description of an empty stomach growling.
  • 粗粝难食 - Description of food being coarse and difficult to eat.
  • 泪涔涔下 - Description of tears flowing continuously.
  • 阿姑守君十余日 - Description of a woman (likely a wife or mother) caring for someone for over ten days.
  • 败屋三椽 - Description of a dilapidated house with three beams.
  • 破衾败絮蓝缕衣裤一堆 - Description of a pile of tattered bedding and clothes.
  • 厨灶亦在房中 - Description of the kitchen being inside the same room.
  • 气息秽不可耐 - Description of the air being foul and unbearable.
  • 居厦屋，役奴仆，衣罗绮，食膏粱 - Description of living in a grand house, having servants, wearing fine clothes, and eating rich food.
  • 判若天渊 - Description of something being as different as heaven and earth.
  • 怦怦懊恼 - Description of someone feeling agitated and annoyed.
  • 求死不得 - Description of someone wanting to die but being unable to do so.
  • 闻老媪唤其妇与孙去 - Description of hearing an elderly woman call her daughter-in-law and grandson to leave.

  • 老媪 - Elderly woman, often used to denote a respected elder.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日 - Means 'the next day,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 殷懃 - Means 'sincerely' or 'earnestly,' conveying the neighbor's earnestness.
  • 親母妻子 - Refers to the mother, wife, and children, emphasizing family members.
  • 視若寇仇 - Means 'to regard as enemies,' indicating a hostile attitude.
  • 鄉黨 - Refers to fellow villagers or neighbors, emphasizing the community.
  • 不齒 - Means 'to hold in contempt,' indicating social ostracism.
  • 顧 - Means 'to care' or 'to look after,' indicating concern.
  • 何以仰事俯育以終乃身乎 - A complex phrase meaning 'how can one support one's parents above and nurture one's children below to live out one's life?' It emphasizes the responsibilities of filial piety and parental duty.
  • 借尸返魂 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to return to life through a borrowed body,' suggesting a rebirth or transformation.
  • 本督抚也，降为杂职 - Means 'originally a governor or provincial inspector, but now reduced to a minor official,' indicating a significant social demotion.
  • 安杂职之分而从其政乎 - Means 'can one not act according to the duties of a minor official?' It emphasizes the need to accept one's current role.
  • 舍此而就尊公 - Means 'to abandon this situation and return to one's father,' indicating a return to a previous state.
  • 面目既非 - Means 'the face is no longer the same,' suggesting a change in appearance or identity.
  • 纵怜而育之 - Means 'even if they feel pity and take care of you,' indicating potential acceptance.
  • 他人必不相容 - Means 'others will definitely not tolerate it,' indicating social rejection.
  • 前生读书作文，曾应童试 - Means 'in a past life, I read and wrote essays, and even took the children's examination,' indicating a previous life of education.
  • 营趁之事，一无所能 - Means 'I am completely incapable of earning a living,' indicating a lack of skills.
  • 游扬闾里，以训童蒙 - Means 'to teach children in the neighborhood,' indicating a new role as a teacher.
  • 自食其力 - Means 'to earn one's own living,' emphasizing self-reliance.
  • 承此一家，以了此躯而已 - Means 'to support this family and complete this life,' indicating the goal of living a fulfilling life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邻翁 - Refers to a neighboring old man, emphasizing the role of an elder in the community.
  • 仕官 - Refers to officials, indicating the social hierarchy.
  • 督抚 - Refers to high-ranking officials, such as governors or provincial inspectors.
  • 杂职 - Refers to minor officials, indicating a lower rank in the bureaucracy.
  • 童试 - Refers to the children's examination, a preliminary examination in the imperial examination system.
  • 闾里 - Refers to the neighborhood or local community.
  • 童蒙 - Refers to young children, emphasizing the role of early education.

  • 绰有余裕 - Means having more than enough resources.
  • 格格不入 - Describes something that does not fit well or is incompatible.
  • 乐而安之 - Describes a state of happiness and contentment.

  • 少君 - A name, possibly referring to a historical figure or a character in a story.

  • 聪敏俊秀 - Describes someone who is intelligent, talented, and handsome, often used to praise a person's qualities.

  • 衰恸而两兄窃喜 - Describes the emotional reactions of the family members, with the older brothers secretly rejoicing.

  • 谈者曰 - Refers to people discussing or commenting on the situation, indicating a broader social context.
  • 不知作何结局矣 - Indicates uncertainty about the future, reflecting the unpredictable nature of life.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 或曰 - Phrase meaning 'someone said' or 'it is said,' introducing a reported opinion or question.
  • 少君之一跌 - Literal phrase meaning 'the fall of Young Master,' where '少君' is a respectful term for a young nobleman or gentleman.
  • 未入幽冥 - Means 'did not enter the underworld,' referring to the afterlife.
  • 不经神判 - Means 'without divine judgment,' indicating that no supernatural intervention occurred.
  • 顿变而为窭人 - Literal phrase meaning 'suddenly changed into a poor person,' where '窭人' refers to a poor or impoverished individual.
  • 何异于膴仕之投荒者 - Means 'how different is this from a wealthy official being exiled?' where '膴仕' refers to a high-ranking official and '投荒' means being banished to a remote place.
  • 造化弄人至此极矣 - Means 'fate has played with people to this extreme,' expressing the idea that fate has been particularly cruel.
  • 不弟之显罚也 - Means 'a clear punishment for a younger brother,' where '不弟' refers to a younger brother who acts improperly.
  • 观其后之不见容于乃兄 - Means 'seeing how he was not accepted by his elder brother,' indicating a lack of acceptance or favor.
  • 即可见前之乃兄受侮不少 - Means 'one can see that his elder brother was greatly humiliated,' suggesting that the elder brother suffered significant humiliation.
  • 若使再世得报，人皆不知 - Means 'if he were to seek revenge in another life, no one would know,' implying that revenge would go unnoticed.
  • 直以现身作法，以示鉴于人伦 - Means 'it is better to live and serve as an example, to show a lesson in human relationships,' emphasizing the importance of living a virtuous life.
  • 一失足成千古恨，再回头已百年身 - Famous saying meaning 'one misstep can lead to regret for a thousand years, and turning back will make you feel like you have lived a hundred lives,' emphasizing the consequences of mistakes.
  • 为少君诵矣 - Means 'this is for Young Master to reflect upon,' indicating that the saying is meant as a lesson for Young Master.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少君 - A respectful term for a young nobleman or gentleman, often used in classical literature.
  • 幽冥 - Refers to the underworld or the afterlife, a common concept in Chinese mythology and religion.
  • 神判 - Refers to divine judgment, a concept often associated with the intervention of gods or spirits in human affairs.
  • 不弟 - Refers to a younger brother who acts improperly, often in a context of family relationships.
  • 人伦 - Refers to human relationships and social norms, a fundamental concept in Confucian ethics.

Proper Nouns

  • 芗厈 - A name or title, possibly a scholar or philosopher, used here to introduce a commentary on the situation.

  • 有云堂幅好者 - A phrase meaning 'someone would say that a scroll was good,' indicating a positive evaluation.
  • 有云单条佳者 - A phrase meaning 'someone would say that a hanging scroll was excellent,' indicating a positive evaluation.
  • 有云横披更妙者 - A phrase meaning 'someone would say that a horizontal hanging scroll was even more wonderful,' indicating a positive evaluation.

  • 所云堂幅者，其家正妻 - A phrase meaning 'the scroll referred to the main wife of the family,' indicating a metaphorical interpretation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 许大宗伯 - Refers to Xu Zhongbo (許汝霖), a high-ranking official during the Ming dynasty.
